./docs/decisions/0014-grooming-project-first.md:5:> **Deploy gap found 2026-07-09 (important):** the first grooming rescope did not take effect in anphabe-gogl because skills live in `~/.claude/skills` + `~/.codex/skills` (installed once), while `onboard --repo-hooks` only refreshes the per-repo vendored `.bee/bin/`. So every SKILL.md prose change since ~0.1.6 (grooming scope, scribing-debt prose, model-tier prose, advisor contract) and the `bee-bypass-gate` skill itself were never on the machine — only the lib mechanics were. **Deploying bee changes needs BOTH: (1) `onboard --repo-hooks` per repo for `.bee/bin/`, and (2) copy `skills/*` → `~/.claude/skills` + `~/.codex/skills`.** Fixed by syncing all 14 skills to both runtimes.
./README.md:302:Requirement: **Node.js 18+** on PATH. One command installs everything — the per-project skills (`.claude/skills/` for Claude Code, `.agents/skills/` for Codex), `CLAUDE.md`, the `AGENTS.md` BEE block, the `.bee/` runtime + vendored helpers, and the Claude Code repo hooks.
./README.md:344:Useful flags: `--global-skills`/`-GlobalSkills` (also install the legacy global copies under `~/.claude/skills` + `~/.codex/skills`), `--no-claude-md`/`-NoClaudeMd`, `--no-hooks`/`-NoHooks`, `--source <local-checkout>`/`-Source`. Full options, the Claude Code plugin route (`/plugin marketplace add thanhsmind/beegog` + `/plugin install bee@bee`), manual installs, uninstall: [INSTALL.md](INSTALL.md).
./README.md:419:Every `--apply` now updates helpers and skills together: by default it syncs the bee skill set into the host repo's own managed roots (`<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` for Codex) from this repo's `skills/` tree in the same run, so helpers and installed skills can no longer drift apart. These trees are committed to the host repo, never gitignored. `--global-skills` additionally syncs the legacy global `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` root; without the flag the global root is never read, written, or deleted. Downgrades are refused by default — if the source tree is older than the repo's vendored helpers or a target's installed skills, apply refuses with zero mutations (`blocked_downgrade`); an unidentifiable source refuses too (`blocked_no_source`), and only `blocked_downgrade` is escapable, via `--force-downgrade`, and only when every version resolves numeric.
./README.md:480:- **Grooming is project-first** (0014) — the hygiene pass hunts the *current project's* debt in plain language; `.bee/`, `.claude/`, `.codex/` and bee's own plumbing are out of scope (a harness bug becomes a one-line upstream note, not a project kill), and the entropy score is demoted to a short hive-housekeeping side-note. Also fixes two real bugs it caught: `capCell` now honors a cell's declared `behavior_change` even when the CLI flag is omitted, and the write-guard no longer misreads `2>&1` as a file write. (Note: this parenthetical is superseded by skill-sync above — `onboard --apply` now syncs `skills/*` into the host repo's own `.claude/skills/bee-*` and `.agents/skills/bee-*` by default, committed to the repo; downgrades refused by default. `--global-skills` extends the sync to the legacy global `~/.claude/skills` root (and, via the install scripts, `~/.codex/skills`); without it neither global root is touched.)
./docs/REFs/Build self-improving agent system with Fable 5 in 14 steps - loops, dynamic workflows, routines.md:301:The compounding contract: **every confirmed lesson goes into a Skill, not just STATE.md**. STATE.md is project-scoped and dies with the project. Skills live in ~/.claude/skills/ and travel with you.
./INSTALL.md:32:| `--global-skills` | `-GlobalSkills` | Also copy skills into the legacy global runtime dirs (`~/.claude/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`). Off by default — see "two layers" below |
./INSTALL.md:50:1. **Repo layer** (once per project, the default): onboarding installs the `AGENTS.md` BEE block, the `.bee/` runtime directory, the vendored `bee.mjs` CLI, a `CLAUDE.md` `@AGENTS.md` import, and a per-project copy of the `bee-*` skills into the repo itself — `<repo>/.claude/skills` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills` for Codex. These skill trees are committed to the host repo (same policy as the vendored CLI), so every teammate and CI job sees identical skills without any machine-wide install; re-onboarding refreshes them.
./INSTALL.md:51:2. **Runtime layer** (opt-in, once per machine): a legacy global copy of the `bee-*` skills into `~/.claude/skills` and/or `~/.codex/skills`. Nothing in this layer is touched unless you pass `--global-skills` (`-GlobalSkills`) — the per-project copy above is what agents actually discover by default. On Claude Code, the hook skeleton still needs one of the routes below (the plugin, or `--repo-hooks` during onboarding).
./INSTALL.md:86:   - or per user (opt-in, legacy): `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\` (macOS/Linux: `~/.claude/skills/`) — pass `--global-skills`/`-GlobalSkills` during onboarding/install to have the script do this too
./INSTALL.md:106:Onboarding (step 3 below) populates the repo-level path by default — no manual step needed. Codex's **repo-level** skill discovery path is `<repo>/.agents/skills/` (cwd up to the repo root), **not** `.codex/skills` — that repo-level location is not a Codex discovery path at all. `~/.codex/skills` (`$CODEX_HOME/skills/`, default `~/.codex/skills/`) is the legacy **global** location; it's opt-in via `--global-skills` and is only populated by the install scripts (`install.sh`/`install.ps1`), not by `onboard_bee.mjs` directly.

./docs/history/codex-harness-hardening-1d/CONTEXT.md:18:| D2 | The 5 kinds by launcher location + manifest: **source_checkout** = canonical dev checkout (plugin.json marker + running from its real `skills/bee-hive`, `identityOk`); **project_projection** = under a host's `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills`; **plugin_package** = an installed manifested package, neither dev checkout nor projection (may source the same repo's runtime+projection but NEVER global/plugin targets — SRC-03); **legacy_global** = the legacy global skills root; **unknown** = missing/unparseable manifest or ambiguous → fail closed before mutation (SRC-04). | Derived from onboard's `HIVE_DIR`/`PLUGIN_ROOT` structure + SRC-02/03/06. ./skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md:31:   - `status: "changes_needed"` → summarize the plan to the user, ask for approval, and only then re-run with `--apply`. Never apply silently. Never replace an existing compact prompt or AGENTS.md content outside the BEE markers without explicit consent. Every `--apply` also syncs the bee skill set into the host repo's two managed roots (`<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` for Codex) in the same run — one command keeps vendored helpers and installed skills at the same version. The trees are committed to the host repo, never gitignored. `--global-skills` additionally syncs the legacy global `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` root; without the flag the global root is never read, written, or deleted. The payload's `skills.targets` carries one entry per target root: `{kind: "repo-claude" | "repo-agents" | "global", target_root, mode, blocked, versions, items}`. When the repo being onboarded contains the running script's own skill tree (bee's own repo), the per-project targets are skipped as a distinct noop (`mode: "self_skip"`) — global sync there is unchanged.

./docs/history/codex-hook-state-parity/CONTEXT.md:31:| D11 | A proven plugin-first migration removes duplicate bee skills **and** installer-managed bee hook entries from the target repository. Skill cleanup is fenced to plain directories named `bee-*` inside `.claude/skills`, `.agents/skills`, and `.codex/skills`; hook cleanup removes only catalog-recognized bee commands from `.claude/settings.json` and `.codex/hooks.json`, preserving all user entries and deleting no container file that still has user content. Symlinks, aliases, files, unknown targets, and paths outside these roots make the cleanup refuse before any mutation. | Enforces D3's exactly-one-source rule for both skills and hooks without deleting runtime configuration owned by the user. |

./docs/history/skill-sync/CONTEXT.md:20:- **ORGANIZE** — layout/mirroring of `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` from the bee repo `skills/`.
./docs/history/skill-sync/CONTEXT.md:26:| D1 | Sync target is the **global** `~/.claude/skills` only — no per-project skill installs | Matches reality: every project shares the global set; host repos carry no local bee skills (checked: anphabe-crm `.claude/skills` has 0 bee skills). One drift source. Per-project pinning deferred until a real need exists |
./docs/history/skill-sync/CONTEXT.md:27:| D2 | Skill sync runs **automatically inside every `onboard_bee.mjs --apply`**, and skill drift appears as plan items in the `--json` dry-run. **Source discovery is deterministic**: the source is the skill tree the running script belongs to — a bee source repo (`skills/bee-*` sibling layout) syncs to the global install; when the script runs from the installed copy itself (`~/.claude/skills/bee-hive/scripts/…`, source root = target root) the skill stage is a **verify-only NOOP**; when no authoritative source resolves, the whole apply **fails closed at preflight** (see D3 — zero mutations). **Authoritative = the tree the running script itself belongs to, anchored on its own `bee-hive`** — within an anchored source, an absent `bee-*` skill IS an intentional removal (D4 mirrors it); a tree the script does not belong to is never guessed at | The failure class was exactly "helpers updated, skills not" (and its reverse). One command = everything consistent; no manual step left to forget. Fresh-eyes finding 2: host-repo onboarding normally runs from the installed copy — self-mirroring or guessing a source there must be impossible by definition |

Installed skills (`~/.claude/skills/bee-swarming/{SKILL.md,references/swarming-reference.md}`) diffed clean against source post-apply. `.claude/settings.json` non-bee keys (`statusLine`, `permissions`, `enabledPlugins`) confirmed byte-identical to the cell-4 snapshot `.bee/workers/mtg-settings-pre.json` before and after apply (file itself untouched by this cell — no PreToolUse change was needed).
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:11:- **E3 — Codex skill discovery (codex-cli 0.144.3, docs at learn.chatgpt.com/docs/build-skills):** repo-level skills load from **`.agents/skills/`** (cwd, parents up to repo root); user-level from `~/.agents/skills`; `~/.codex/skills` still scanned (legacy — bee skills there DO appear in `codex debug prompt-input` on WSL). Repo-level `.codex/skills` is **not** a documented discovery path.
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:12:- **E4 — Claude Code discovery (code.claude.com/docs/en/skills):** project skills load from `.claude/skills/` (starting dir → repo root, nested dirs on demand); personal from `~/.claude/skills`. `.agents/skills` is NOT a Claude Code discovery path. Symlinked skill dirs are followed by both runtimes, but Windows symlinks need privileges → real copies are the portable choice.
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:14:- **E6 — onboard_bee.mjs already has the full D1-D5 skill-sync machinery** (`computeSkillItems(sourceRoot, targetRoot)`, alias/symlink guards, version preflight) parameterized by target root — currently hardwired to `~/.claude/skills` (`skillsTargetRoot()`, line 218). Skills payload ≈ 1.9 MB / 15 dirs.
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:19:- **D2 — Per-project skill install is the default.** Onboarding syncs the bee skill set into the **host repo**: `<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` (Claude Code) and `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` (Codex). Same D1-D5 sync machinery, target roots now repo-relative; the repo/target overlap guard is re-derived for in-repo targets (the repo *contains* the target by design — the guard must exempt the managed subdirs while still refusing when the repo root IS the skills source checkout, i.e. beegog itself never self-installs per-project copies over its own `skills/`).
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:20:- **D3 — Global install becomes opt-in.** New flag `--global-skills` (sh: `--global-skills`, ps1: `-GlobalSkills`) restores the old behavior (`~/.claude/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`). Existing global copies are never deleted by this change; drift against them is no longer managed once a repo goes per-project (surfaced as an INSTALL.md note).
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:23:- **D6 — Codex bootstrap path is `.agents/skills` + AGENTS.md BEE block.** No `.codex/skills` repo dir (not a discovery path). `~/.codex/skills` legacy copy only under `--global-skills`. INSTALL.md rewritten to describe the per-project layout for both runtimes.
./docs/history/installer-hardening/CONTEXT.md:24:- **D7 — Acceptance = airemote.** Re-onboard `goglbe/airemote` with the new flow and verify: CLAUDE.md present with import; `.claude/skills` + `.agents/skills` populated (15 bee-*); `codex debug prompt-input` lists bee skills from the repo path; `node .bee/bin/bee_status.mjs` healthy; `powershell.exe` parse check on install.ps1 reports zero errors.

./docs/history/p49-force-downgrade-blast-radius/reports/advisor-digest.md:21:Fix shape: add a `host_items` array — repoRoot-relative `{action: "copy_lib"|"copy_helper", path}` filtered from the already-computed `plan` — to the refused-apply blocked response when the block is forceable, mirroring the existing D2 forced-apply-transparency precedent for skill items (`computeSkillSyncTarget()` :883-894). No change to guard logic, block conditions, or what a forced apply executes. ./docs/history/p49-force-downgrade-blast-radius/reports/advisor-digest.md:55:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.agents/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md" in /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog
./docs/history/p49-force-downgrade-blast-radius/reports/advisor-digest.md:87:   - `status: "changes_needed"` → summarize the plan to the user, ask for approval, and only then re-run with `--apply`. Never apply silently. Never replace an existing compact prompt or AGENTS.md content outside the BEE markers without explicit consent. Every `--apply` also syncs the bee skill set into the host repo's two managed roots (`<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` for Codex) in the same run — one command keeps vendored helpers and installed skills at the same version. The trees are committed to the host repo, never gitignored. `--global-skills` additionally syncs the legacy global `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` root; without the flag the global root is never read, written, or deleted. The payload's `skills.targets` carries one entry per target root: `{kind: "repo-claude" | "repo-agents" | "global", target_root, mode, blocked, versions, items}`. When the repo being onboarded contains the running script's own skill tree (bee's own repo), the per-project targets are skipped as a distinct noop (`mode: "self_skip"`) — global sync there is unchanged.

./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/approach.md:33:S1 → S2 → (S3, S4, S6 parallelizable) → S5 → S7. S5 touches `onboard_bee.mjs` skill-sync — the second-highest blast-radius surface after `resolveRoots`; it gets the frozen-green regression net before any renderer change. Every rendered artifact (`.codex/hooks.json`, `.claude/skills/*`, `.agents/skills/*`, agent files) is edited at its template/generator source; the generator drift check (1.5.0) is the enforcement backstop.
./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/CONTEXT.md:28:- **D9 — Adapter split for the 5 runtime-sensitive skills** (`bee-hive`, `bee-swarming`, `bee-executing`, `bee-validating`, `bee-reviewing`): shared core + per-runtime adapter fragments, rendered at onboarding sync into runtime-specific SKILL.md per managed root (`.claude/skills/` gets core+claude, `.agents/skills/` gets core+codex). Root `skills/` remains the single human-edited source (core + `adapters/` subdirs); no parallel `dist/` tree. Sync/mirror/release tests learn the rendering; a semantic-hash check keeps shared cores identical across runtimes. The 10 workflow-semantic skills stay unsplit.

./docs/history/grill-deltas/reports/review.md:20:- Artifacts EXISTS/SUBSTANTIVE/WIRED: materiality test present in exploring step 4 + Red Flags; `## Terms` section in context-template with pin guidance; scribing input table maps Terms → Data Dictionary; installed `~/.claude/skills` copies byte-identical (diff -q clean).
./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-bundle-s3.md:7:codex-native-runtime-v2 slices 1-2 shipped (docs truth, atomic-write hardening, update_plan matcher superset, capability matrix on codex-cli 0.144.4, permission/profile docs, manifest regen + byte pin, Codex spawn_agent guard with isolated fail-open branch). Slice 3 is the adapter split (D9, refined by logged decision): the 5 runtime-sensitive skills get inline runtime-conditional blocks — `<!-- bee:only claude -->…<!-- bee:end -->` / `<!-- bee:only codex -->…<!-- bee:end -->` — and the onboarding skill-sync renders each managed repo root by filtering (`.claude/skills` drops codex blocks, `.agents/skills` drops claude blocks, markers stripped; unmarked = shared core). skills/ stays the single human-edited source; plugin routes keep shipping source for now (pre-render at release filed as follow-up PBI).

Per-target version/drift hashing moves to rendered bytes. Must resolve how bee's own repo mirrors (.claude/skills, .agents/skills — currently byte-identical to skills/, kept by a mechanism the worker must find, self_skip claimed for onboarding) receive rendered copies.

./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s3.md:949:  "action": "Per D9 (refined — see decision log: inline runtime blocks): BEFORE any renderer change, freeze the regression net — run the full recorded verify chain and record it green (critical pattern 20260716). Then teach the onboarding skill-sync stage in onboard_bee.mjs a runtime-conditional filter applied when copying each skill file into a managed repo root: lines between <!-- bee:only claude --> and <!-- bee:end --> survive only into the .claude/skills target; <!-- bee:only codex --> blocks survive only into .agents/skills; marker lines themselves are always stripped from rendered output; unmarked content is shared core and copies to both. Nesting is refused (a bee:only inside a bee:only is a hard validation error at sync time, loud, zero writes for that skill); an unclosed marker likewise. Decide and implement how per-target version markers/hashes account for rendered content (each target hashes its OWN rendered bytes — never the source bytes — so drift detection stays honest per root), and how bee's own repo gets rendered copies given self_skip (read the current mechanism that keeps .claude/skills + .agents/skills byte-identical to skills/ in THIS repo and extend it to render — likely the same sync path host repos use; find it, do not guess). Write scripts/test_skill_render.mjs: unit tests for the filter (claude-only block, codex-only block, both, none, marker stripping, nesting refusal, unclosed refusal, idempotent re-render) plus an integration assertion that a fixture skill with blocks renders differently per target and identically on re-run. Update test_onboard_bee.mjs / test_plugin_distribution.mjs expectations ONLY where the render model legitimately changes them — document each changed expectation in the cell report. No skill content is tagged in this cell (that is cnr2-10/11); with zero markers present, rendered output MUST be byte-identical to source — assert that explicitly (the no-marker no-op invariant is the regression net).",

./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s3.md:3139:.bee/cells/cnr2-9.json:22:  "action": "Per D9 (refined — see decision log: inline runtime blocks): BEFORE any renderer change, freeze the regression net — run the full recorded verify chain and record it green (critical pattern 20260716). Then teach the onboarding skill-sync stage in onboard_bee.mjs a runtime-conditional filter applied when copying each skill file into a managed repo root: lines between <!-- bee:only claude --> and <!-- bee:end --> survive only into the .claude/skills target; <!-- bee:only codex --> blocks survive only into .agents/skills; marker lines themselves are always stripped from rendered output; unmarked content is shared core and copies to both. Nesting is refused (a bee:only inside a bee:only is a hard validation error at sync time, loud, zero writes for that skill); an unclosed marker likewise. Decide and implement how per-target version markers/hashes account for rendered content (each target hashes its OWN rendered bytes — never the source bytes — so drift detection stays honest per root), and how bee's own repo gets rendered copies given self_skip (read the current mechanism that keeps .claude/skills + .agents/skills byte-identical to skills/ in THIS repo and extend it to render — likely the same sync path host repos use; find it, do not guess). Write scripts/test_skill_render.mjs: unit tests for the filter (claude-only block, codex-only block, both, none, marker stripping, nesting refusal, unclosed refusal, idempotent re-render) plus an integration assertion that a fixture skill with blocks renders differently per target and identically on re-run. Update test_onboard_bee.mjs / test_plugin_distribution.mjs expectations ONLY where the render model legitimately changes them — document each changed expectation in the cell report. No skill content is tagged in this cell (that is cnr2-10/11); with zero markers present, rendered output MUST be byte-identical to source — assert that explicitly (the no-marker no-op invariant is the regression net).",

./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest.md:124:   - `status: "changes_needed"` → summarize the plan to the user, ask for approval, and only then re-run with `--apply`. Never apply silently. Never replace an existing compact prompt or AGENTS.md content outside the BEE markers without explicit consent. Every `--apply` also syncs the bee skill set into the host repo's two managed roots (`<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` for Codex) in the same run — one command keeps vendored helpers and installed skills at the same version. The trees are committed to the host repo, never gitignored. `--global-skills` additionally syncs the legacy global `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` root; without the flag the global root is never read, written, or deleted. The payload's `skills.targets` carries one entry per target root: `{kind: "repo-claude" | "repo-agents" | "global", target_root, mode, blocked, versions, items}`. When the repo being onboarded contains the running script's own skill tree (bee's own repo), the per-project targets are skipped as a distinct noop (`mode: "self_skip"`) — global sync there is unchanged.

./docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/ao-2aii-2.md:5:**Outcome:** The Delegation contract (`routing-and-contracts.md`) gained the cli gather branch: when `resolveTier(root, 'generation', runtime, {for:'gather'}).type === 'cli'`, a gather runs the configured command verbatim (nothing appended), prompt on stdin, every worker-facing path absolute, read-only by contract. Stdout **is** the digest, framed by a `<<<BEE_DIGEST … BEE_DIGEST>>>` delimiter contract; missing delimiters or an empty digest is a failed run, surfaced loudly, never a silent green. No `result.json`/cell/reservation/worker registration for a gather. The known `dispatch.jsonl` measurement gap (a Bash-launched gather emits zero rows) is named and handed to Slice 3. `AGENTS.block.md` critical rule 13 gained one added sentence (the cli-gather transport rider); root `AGENTS.md` was re-rendered via the repo's own self-onboard script to match — that run also synced this repo's own installed bee-hive mirrors (`.agents/skills`, `.claude/skills`) and `.bee/onboarding.json`'s managed-hash record, clearing drift left over from `ao-2aii-1`. Census anchors added to `test_lib.mjs`.
./docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/ao-2aii-2.md:7:**Files touched:** `skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md`, `skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs`, `docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json`, plus (deviation, see below) `.agents/skills/bee-hive/{references/routing-and-contracts.md,templates/AGENTS.block.md,templates/lib/state.mjs,templates/tests/test_lib.mjs}`, `.claude/skills/bee-hive/{references/routing-and-contracts.md,templates/AGENTS.block.md,templates/lib/state.mjs,templates/tests/test_lib.mjs}`, `.bee/onboarding.json`.
./docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/ao-2aii-2.md:11:**Deviations:** the cell's `files` list did not name the `.agents/skills/bee-hive/*` / `.claude/skills/bee-hive/*` installed mirrors or `.bee/onboarding.json`, but the cell action explicitly mandates re-rendering `AGENTS.md` via the repo's own onboard script, and this repo self-hosts bee-hive. Running that mandated command mechanically synced the installed mirrors from the edited source templates (surfacing and clearing pre-existing drift in `templates/lib/state.mjs` left unsynced by `ao-2aii-1`) and updated `.bee/onboarding.json`'s managed-hash record. Included in this cell's commit as a direct, deterministic byproduct of the one instructed command — not an out-of-scope hand-edit. `docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md` and `.bee/decisions.jsonl` also showed as modified in `git status` but predate this cell's work (prior gate-approval logging) and were deliberately left unstaged.
./docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/validation-slice-2a.md:15:| REPO FIT | **FAIL** | The slice edits `skills/` (source), but the runtime loads `~/.claude/skills/bee-*`, which is **already stale**. No cell syncs it, and `ao-2b` edits the *template* `AGENTS.block.md` while the loaded standing order is the **rendered** `AGENTS.md:55` — a file in no cell. The contract would have shipped without taking effect. |
./docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/ao-3b-1.md:13:Self-onboarded this repo (`onboard_bee.mjs --apply`): `.claude/agents/bee-{gather,extract,review}.md` now exist, rendered from the live config (generation=sonnet, extraction=haiku, review=opus); the `bee-hive` skill mirror sync carried the new templates/script changes into `.claude/skills/bee-hive` and `.agents/skills/bee-hive` automatically (generic recursive tree sync, no new code needed for the mirrors). Release manifest regenerated via `--write` (146 files).
./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:73:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.agents/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md" in /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog
./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:105:   - `status: "changes_needed"` → summarize the plan to the user, ask for approval, and only then re-run with `--apply`. Never apply silently. Never replace an existing compact prompt or AGENTS.md content outside the BEE markers without explicit consent. Every `--apply` also syncs the bee skill set into the host repo's two managed roots (`<repo>/.claude/skills/bee-*` for Claude Code, `<repo>/.agents/skills/bee-*` for Codex) in the same run — one command keeps vendored helpers and installed skills at the same version. The trees are committed to the host repo, never gitignored. `--global-skills` additionally syncs the legacy global `~/.claude/skills/bee-*` root; without the flag the global root is never read, written, or deleted. The payload's `skills.targets` carries one entry per target root: `{kind: "repo-claude" | "repo-agents" | "global", target_root, mode, blocked, versions, items}`. When the repo being onboarded contains the running script's own skill tree (bee's own repo), the per-project targets are skipped as a distinct noop (`mode: "self_skip"`) — global sync there is unchanged.

./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:1233:docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/approach.md:33:S1 → S2 → (S3, S4, S6 parallelizable) → S5 → S7. S5 touches `onboard_bee.mjs` skill-sync — the second-highest blast-radius surface after `resolveRoots`; it gets the frozen-green regression net before any renderer change. Every rendered artifact (`.codex/hooks.json`, `.claude/skills/*`, `.agents/skills/*`, agent files) is edited at its template/generator source; the generator drift check (1.5.0) is the enforcement backstop.
./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:1250:docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/CONTEXT.md:28:- **D9 — Adapter split for the 5 runtime-sensitive skills** (`bee-hive`, `bee-swarming`, `bee-executing`, `bee-validating`, `bee-reviewing`): shared core + per-runtime adapter fragments, rendered at onboarding sync into runtime-specific SKILL.md per managed root (`.claude/skills/` gets core+claude, `.agents/skills/` gets core+codex). Root `skills/` remains the single human-edited source (core + `adapters/` subdirs); no parallel `dist/` tree. Sync/mirror/release tests learn the rendering; a semantic-hash check keeps shared cores identical across runtimes. The 10 workflow-semantic skills stay unsplit.

lanes: a global HANDOFF blocks a lane start only when its feature names this lane; the DEFAULT start keeps any-handoff-blocks\nPASS  lanes: a registered worker blocks a lane start only when its cell buildSessionPreamble: a bound sessionId shows that lane's own phase/mode/feature/gates and names other ACTIVE lanes in one line — never the bound lane itself, never a terminal one\nPASS  buildSessionPreamble: an unresolvable binding (missing lane) falls back to the default record instead of blocking the informational preamble\nPASS  buildPromptReminder: omitting sessionId is ./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:1261:docs/specs/onboarding.md:6:  - installer-version-parity-1-3-1 locked rules (fail-closed release tuple, full projection parity, greenfield/brownfield end-to-end success contract; D1/D3 verified in-engine with plugin-first coverage, D7 managed-set cleanup fencing, D2/D8 Linux Bash E2E shipped — cells -4/-2/-3, 2026-07-16; field fix cell -5: plugin CLI mutation verbs take NO --json (only `plugin list` does — real codex/claude contract), rollback reconciles the probed current state against the pre-run snapshot and reports failure only when restoring a previously-present plugin genuinely fails (a transition that died before installing anything rolls back as a no-op success), and the E2E fake CLIs reject --json on mutations so the wrong-flag contract can never test green again; field fix cell -6: legacy global skill copies (the pre-1.0 `~/.claude/skills` layout) are refreshed IN PLACE on every onboard — refresh-only over managed bee-* dirs that already exist there (never create, never delete, non-managed dirs untouched, resolved-newer global never downgraded, self/overlap sources skipped), reported as `refresh_legacy_global_skill` plan items that never drive `up_to_date`/blocked-first aggregation — closing the two-version skill inconsistency where a repo at the current release coexisted with a stale global copy both loaded by the runtime)

lanes: resolvePipeline — a binding to a missing or corrupt lane is a TYPED refusal naming the lane, never a silent fall-back to the default\nPASS  lanes: startFeature lane mode creates the lane with agent name still governs the swarming branch as before)\nPASS  checkWrite: a present-but-corrupt reservation store RETURNS a typed {allow:false, kind:\"holds-unreadable\"} verdict for a session-aware write — never a throw (C7, panel B1); a missing store stays open exactly as today\nPASS  buildSessionPreamble: omitting sessionId (or passing {}) renders byte-identical to today; an unbound session ./docs/history/codex-native-runtime-v2/reports/advisor-digest-s2.md:1629:    28	- **D9 — Adapter split for the 5 runtime-sensitive skills** (`bee-hive`, `bee-swarming`, `bee-executing`, `bee-validating`, `bee-reviewing`): shared core + per-runtime adapter fragments, rendered at onboarding sync into runtime-specific SKILL.md per managed root (`.claude/skills/` gets core+claude, `.agents/skills/` gets core+codex). Root `skills/` remains the single human-edited source (core + `adapters/` subdirs); no parallel `dist/` tree. # Context: Installer Version Parity 1.3.1

## Boundary

Fix the two top-level installers so a successful greenfield or brownfield onboarding cannot leave Codex, Claude, the vendored runtime, or project-local skills on different bee versions. Ship the completed Codex sandbox and hook-state parity work as release 1.3.1 while excluding unfinished wait-loop and worktree-isolation changes.

## Locked decisions

- **D1 — fail closed on one release tuple.** Every authoritative source marker required to perform an install must exist, be readable, and agree before target mutation. Target runtime/onboarding/project projections may be absent on greenfield input, but every applicable target surface must exist and equal the validated source tuple before success. An existing unreadable or unequal target refuses or is upgraded through an explicitly proven path; it is never ignored. Decision `55ff17ef`.
- **D2 — prove the wrappers, not only their helpers.** Both installers must be exercised through greenfield and brownfield entry flows. Success requires the requested version, complete onboarding information, no managed drift, preserved owner content/state, and an immediate `up_to_date` repeat. Decision `09b776b5`.
- **D3 — repair source-repository self-onboarding.** A canonical source checkout may not retain stale discoverable `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills` projections. Self-skip cannot exclude those projections from update or truth reporting.
- **D4 — release boundary.** Release 1.3.1 includes capped `codex-sandbox-baseline`, capped `codex-hook-state-parity`, this fix, and their truthful specs/history. It excludes every `codex-agent-wait-loop` and `worktree-isolation` source or bookkeeping hunk that is not independently completed. Decision `fc76ce41`.
- **D5 — cross-platform proof.** Linux execution is required locally. Windows PowerShell execution must run on a real Windows/PowerShell environment; ASCII/token checks are not a substitute. If unavailable, release is blocked rather than called proven.
- **D6 — release publication.** After the immutable reviewed scope is green, bump the complete version tuple to `1.3.1`, regenerate the release inventory, self-onboard, commit on `main`, tag `v1.3.1`, and push `main` plus the tag.
- **D7 — exact project cleanup ownership.** Plugin-first cleanup may remove only project fallback skills proven to be members of bee's managed release set. A project-owned directory such as `bee-custom` survives even though its name shares the prefix.
- **D8 — no pre-confirmation runtime mutation.** Wrapper planning and dry-run never install, remove, or alter runtime plugins. Tests exercise plugin transitions through PATH-isolated fake CLIs with call logs and deny sentinels; a fixture state file cannot substitute for this ordering proof.

## Success conditions

- A stale 0.1.43 project projection upgraded from a 1.3.1 source becomes 1.3.1 everywhere and a second run reports no work.
- A deliberately mixed release tuple is rejected before target mutation by both entry points.
- New and existing projects receive all required onboarding records and managed artifacts for the chosen distribution mode.
- Linux and Windows entrypoint tests execute, not merely parse or grep the scripts.
- The release diff contains no open wait-loop/worktree-isolation work.
- Project-owned `bee-*` skills outside the proven managed release set survive plugin-first cleanup.
- Plugin install/removal occurs only after confirmation and a repeatable preflight snapshot.

## Terms

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| release tuple | The version asserted by every authoritative package/runtime marker that must move together. |
| project projection | A project-local copy of bee skills discoverable by an assistant runtime. |
| complete onboarding | Required managed records and artifacts exist, their fingerprints match live files, status reports no drift, and a repeat check is current. |

## Out of scope

- Completing wait-loop or worktree-isolation features.
- Redesigning the plugin manager CLIs.
- Treating an unavailable Windows runner or read-only Git metadata as a passing result.

  1752	
  1753	// ---------- codex hooks (.codex/hooks.json) ----------
  1754	// The Codex projection of the same repo-hook set, mirroring hooks/catalog.mjs
  1755	// TARGETS.REPO but with host-repo paths: Codex never sets $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
  1756	// (the Claude-only variable above), so every command resolves the git root
  1757	// from the session cwd and fails open VISIBLY when there is none. Two pinned
  1758	// differences from renderRepoHookEntries(), both from hooks/catalog.mjs:
  1759	//   - bee-model-guard.mjs is wired on a DIFFERENT matcher per runtime: Claude
  1760	//     guards Agent|Task, Codex guards spawn_agent (the collaboration-spawn tool
  1761	//     name Codex exposes through PreToolUse — capability-matrix row D1). Same
  1762	//     handler, only the matcher differs (ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES).
  1763	//   - each entry carries a statusMessage (Codex TUI shows it while running).
  1764	
  1765	const CODEX_TRANSPORT_DIAGNOSTIC = "bee: hook transport unavailable (no git root)";
  1766	
  1767	// A repo that ships hooks/catalog.mjs IS bee, and that catalog — not this file —
  1768	// is the authority for its own .codex/hooks.json (TARGETS.REPO renders
  1769	// `"$r"/hooks/<script>`, because bee's hooks live in hooks/). renderCodexHookEntries()
  1770	// below is the HOST projection: it points at .bee/bin/hooks/, correct for a host repo
  1771	// (which has no hooks/ dir, only the vendored copy) and WRONG for bee itself. Writing
  1772	// it into bee's own repo silently clobbers the catalog rendering and turns
  1773	// hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs red on the NEXT run — which is exactly how a release
  1774	// broke: the drift check fired, the file was "repaired" by hand, and the next
  1775	// self-onboard undid the repair. Two renderers, one generated file; this is the
  1776	// self-skip the skill sync already does (mode "self_skip"), keyed on the file that
  1777	// makes a repo the catalog's owner rather than on where the script happens to run from.
  1778	function repoOwnsHookCatalog(repoRoot) {
  1779	  return fs.existsSync(path.join(repoRoot, "hooks", "catalog.mjs"));
  1780	}
  1781	
  1782	function codexHookCommand(fileName) {
  1783	  return [
  1784	    'r="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"',
  1785	    `[ -n "$r" ] || { echo "${CODEX_TRANSPORT_DIAGNOSTIC}" >&2; exit 0; }`,
  1786	    `exec node "$r"/.bee/bin/hooks/${fileName} --source=repo`,
  1787	  ].join("\n");
  1788	}
  1789	
  1790	function renderCodexHookEntries() {
  1791	  const entry = (fileName, statusMessage) => ({
  1792	    type: "command",
  1793	    command: codexHookCommand(fileName),
  1794	    statusMessage,
  1795	  });
  1796	  return {
  1797	    SessionStart: [
  1798	      {
  1799	        matcher: "startup|resume|clear|compact",
  1800	        hooks: [entry("bee-session-init.mjs", "bee: session bootstrap")],
  1801	      },
  1802	    ],
  1803	    UserPromptSubmit: [{ hooks: [entry("bee-prompt-context.mjs", "bee: phase reminder")] }],
  1804	    PreToolUse: [
  1805	      {
  1806	        matcher: "Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash|Read|Glob|Grep|AskUserQuestion",
  1807	        hooks: [entry("bee-write-guard.mjs", "bee: write guard")],
  1808	      },
  1809	      {
  1810	        // Codex-native spawn guard (codex-native-runtime-v2 D4): Codex exposes
  1811	        // agent spawns as tool_name "spawn_agent"; bee-model-guard.mjs runs an
  1812	        // isolated Codex branch on the observed envelope. Claude wires the same
  1813	        // handler on Agent|Task (renderRepoHookEntries) — matcher differs only.
  1814	        matcher: "spawn_agent",
  1815	        hooks: [entry("bee-model-guard.mjs", "bee: model-tier guard")],
  1660	function hashTree(dir) {
  1661	  if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) {
  1662	    return "ABSENT";
  1663	  }
  1664	  const lines = [];
  1665	  const walk = (d, prefix) => {
  1666	    const entries = fs.readdirSync(d, { withFileTypes: true })
  1667	      .sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));
  1668	    for (const e of entries) {
  1669	      const rel = prefix ? `${prefix}/${e.name}` : e.name;
  1670	      const abs = path.join(d, e.name);
  1671	      if (e.isSymbolicLink()) {
  1672	        lines.push(`link ${rel} -> ${fs.readlinkSync(abs)}`);
  1673	      } else if (e.isDirectory()) {
  1674	        lines.push(`dir ${rel}`);
  1675	        walk(abs, rel);
  1676	      } else {
  1677	        lines.push(
  1678	          `file ${rel} ${crypto.createHash("sha256").update(fs.readFileSync(abs)).digest("hex")}`);
  1679	      }
  1680	    }
  1681	  };
  1682	  walk(dir, "");
  1683	  return lines.join("\n");
  1684	}

c77e38d feat(ao-5-1): AO14 — tiny/small execute via one dispatched execution worker
832cb63 release(installer-version-parity-1-3-1): bee 1.3.1 — fail-closed installer parity
a4355ab Update
commit c77e38d8a21b931c3c5a7dcb2edd623f572df618
Author: Thành Nguyễn <nguyenphuongthanhf@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jul 17 23:54:28 2026 +0700

    feat(ao-5-1): AO14 — tiny/small execute via one dispatched execution worker
    
    bee-hive's lane table names a dispatched execution worker (param-carrying,
    never a bare marker) for tiny/small Execute instead of "direct, in-session
    (solo)"; bee-swarming's Solo execution section becomes Single execution
    worker (registers in the swarm registry, takes reservations, single worker
    under the standard execution contract, no wave/reviewers/panels); the
    orchestrator authors the tiny/small done-report from the worker's verbatim
    diff plus its own independent verify re-run. The Delegation contract in
    routing-and-contracts.md gains the execution-worker class beside the
    I/O-offload worker, distinguished by authority and state effects. AGENTS.block.md's
    rule-13 parenthetical carries the AO14 rider and root AGENTS.md is
    re-rendered via self-onboard. Stale "solo execution" cross-refs in
    bee-swarming/bee-planning/bee-reviewing are updated; one new census row in
    test_lib.mjs pins the class and the retired phrasing; release manifest
    regenerated.
    
    Cell ao-5-1.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/.claude/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md b/.claude/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md
index ea2461d..e12611d 100644
--- a/.claude/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md
+++ b/.claude/skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md
@@ -113,8 +113,8 @@ Review is on demand (SPEC R1/R3/R8, decision 565e68d0): no lane auto-dispatches
 | Lane | Plan | Validate | Execute | Review | Human stops |
 |---|---|---|---|---|---|
 | `docs` | none — announce one line | format check (parse/lint if applicable) | direct, in-session | none | 0 |
-| `tiny` | short `plan.md` direct note | 2-minute reality check inline, 0 ceremony subagents (I/O-offload workers exempt — Delegation contract) | direct, in-session (solo) | self-review + done-report (diff + fresh verify output) — unchanged, this is verification, not independent review | 1 — the merged shape+execution gate |
-| `small` | short `plan.md` | inline reality gate + matrix, 0 ceremony subagents (I/O-offload workers exempt — Delegation contract); spike only if a blocking assumption demands it | direct, in-session (solo) | self-checks only, no auto reviewer (the correctness reviewer moves inside an on-demand review session) | 2 — merged shape+execution gate, self-checks close-out |
+| `tiny` | short `plan.md` direct note | 2-minute reality check inline, 0 ceremony subagents (I/O-offload workers exempt — Delegation contract) | one dispatched execution worker (AO14 — param-carrying dispatch, model param or pinned type, never a bare marker; standard worker prompt template, no reviewers/panels/waves) | orchestrator-authored done-report (worker's verbatim diff + orchestrator's own fresh verify re-run) — unchanged, this is verification, not independent review | 1 — the merged shape+execution gate |
+| `small` | short `plan.md` | inline reality gate + matrix, 0 ceremony subagents (I/O-offload workers exempt — Delegation contract); spike only if a blocking assumption demands it | one dispatched execution worker (AO14 — same contract as `tiny`'s Execute column) | orchestrator-authored done-report, self-checks only, no auto reviewer (the correctness reviewer moves inside an on-demand review session) | 2 — merged shape+execution gate, self-checks close-out |
 | `standard` | full `plan.md` | plan-checker + cell reviewer | swarm workers | on user request only: session panel scaled to scope risk (4 core reviewers) | 3 — Gates 1-3 |
 | `high-risk` | `plan.md` + brief | persona panel | swarm workers | on user request only: session panel scaled to scope risk (full wave + conditionals) | 3 — Gates 1-3 |
 
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@ Review is on demand (SPEC R1/R3/R8, decision 565e68d0): no lane auto-dispatches
 
 **Docs lane:** the change is knowledge upkeep, same class as capture — announce one line ("docs lane: writing X"), write it, run a format check when one exists (JSON parses, markdown lints), log a decision/capture stub when the content encodes a settled outcome. No cells, no gates, no reviewers. If the target path is outside the write-guard allowlist (`.bee/, docs/, plans/, AGENTS.md`) the hook will block the idle write — fall back to the tiny fast path instead of fighting the guard.
 
-**Tiny fast path:** Gates 2 and 3 are presented as **one merged question** — "Work shape + execution: I'm about to do X via Y, verified by Z. Approve?" — approval records both `shape` and `execution`. The 2-minute reality check runs inline before that question (validating folds into planning; it does not disappear). After the work: no separate merge gate — the done-report (diff + fresh verify output + capture line) closes it. A real problem found during self-review stops and asks, always.
+**Tiny fast path:** Gates 2 and 3 are presented as **one merged question** — "Work shape + execution: I'm about to do X via Y, verified by Z. Approve?" — approval records both `shape` and `execution`. The 2-minute reality check runs inline before that question (validating folds into planning; it does not disappear). Implementation itself runs through the one dispatched execution worker named in the Execute column above (AO14) — never in-session. After the worker returns: no separate merge gate — the orchestrator authors the done-report itself (the worker's verbatim diff plus the orchestrator's own independent verify re-run, never the worker's word) and that done-report (diff + fresh verify output + capture line) closes it. A real problem found during the orchestrator's own review stops and asks, always.
 
 ## The Four Gates

   122	  } catch (error) {
   123	    fail(`release manifest regeneration failed: ${error.message}`);
   124	  }
   125	}
   126	
   127	// ─── remaining release steps (already automated, listed for the operator) ──
   128	console.log(`
   129	Version set to ${newVersion}. Remaining release steps:
   130	  1. Propagate projections + onboarding ledger:
   131	       node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply
   132	     (refreshes .claude/ and .agents/ skill copies and .bee/onboarding.json;
   133	      the split-brain test anchors self-update — no hand edit.)
   134	  2. Run the full verify suite (must be green).
   135	  3. Commit, then tag: git tag v${newVersion} && git push --tags
   136	`);

 succeeded in 217ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	// Release manifest generator + checker (DIST-01/DIST-03/D-03, decision ed0b2920).
     3	//
     4	// Enumerates the release-identity file set for the bee distribution:
     5	//   - skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/*.mjs   -> role "source_lib"
     6	//   - .bee/bin/lib/*.mjs                    -> role "runtime_lib"
     7	//   - skills/** and hooks/**                 -> canonical plugin package
     8	//   - both plugin manifests + marketplace   -> plugin metadata
     9	//   - both installers + distribution engine -> migration machinery
    10	//
    11	// The lib directories are enumerated via fs.readdirSync — never a hand-kept
    12	// list (crit-pattern 20260714: hand-kept file lists silently drift from the
    13	// real tree). The two plugin.json files are individually named because they
    14	// are not part of an enumerable lib directory.
    15	//
    16	// Subcommands:
    17	//   --write     regenerate docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json
    18	//   --check     recompute the manifest from the current tree and compare it
    19	//               against the stored one; prints each mismatch; exit 1 on any
    20	//               path-set/hash/mode diff, exit 0 when identical
    21	//   --selftest  proves the comparison logic actually bites: takes the real
    22	//               (unmutated) manifest as a baseline, mutates ONE covered
    23	//               file's content in a temp copy (never the real tree), and
    24	//               asserts compareManifests() flags exactly that file. Exit 0
    25	//               if the bite is proven, exit 1 if not.
    26	
    27	import fs from "node:fs";
    28	import path from "node:path";
    29	import os from "node:os";
    30	import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
    31	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
    32	
    33	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    34	const REPO_ROOT = path.join(__dirname, "..");
    35	const MANIFEST_PATH = path.join(
    36	  REPO_ROOT,
    37	  "docs",
    38	  "history",
    39	  "codex-harness-hardening",
    40	  "release-manifest.json",
    41	);
    42	
    43	const RUNTIME_LIB_DIR = path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".bee", "bin", "lib");
    44	const NAMED_PLUGIN_MANIFESTS = [
    45	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".claude-plugin", "plugin.json"),
    46	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".codex-plugin", "plugin.json"),
    47	];
    48	const PLUGIN_MARKETPLACE = path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".claude-plugin", "marketplace.json");
    49	const DISTRIBUTION_TOOLS = [
    50	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, "scripts", "install.sh"),
    51	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, "scripts", "install.ps1"),
    52	];
    53	const DISTRIBUTION_TESTS = [
    54	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, "scripts", "test_verify_manifest.mjs"),
    55	  path.join(REPO_ROOT, "scripts", "test_release_tuple.mjs"),
    56	];
    57	
    58	const SCHEMA_VERSION = 1;
    59	
    60	/** repo-relative POSIX path for an absolute path under REPO_ROOT. */
    61	function relPosix(absPath) {
    62	  return path.relative(REPO_ROOT, absPath).split(path.sep).join("/");
    63	}
    64	
    65	function sha256File(absPath) {
    66	  const data = fs.readFileSync(absPath);
    67	  return createHash("sha256").update(data).digest("hex");
    68	}
    69	
    70	function modeOctal(absPath) {
    71	  const mode = fs.statSync(absPath).mode & 0o777;
    72	  return mode.toString(8).padStart(3, "0");
    73	}
    74	
    75	function buildRecord(absPath, role, packagePath = null) {
    76	  const record = {
    77	    path: relPosix(absPath),
    78	    sha256: sha256File(absPath),
    79	    mode: modeOctal(absPath),
    80	    role,
    81	  };
    82	  if (packagePath) record.packagePath = packagePath;
    83	  return record;
    84	}
    85	
    86	function enumerateTree(dirAbsPath, role) {
    87	  if (!fs.existsSync(dirAbsPath)) throw new Error(`release_manifest: expected directory missing: ${dirAbsPath}`);
    88	  const records = [];
    89	  const walk = (current) => {
    90	    for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true })) {
    91	      const absPath = path.join(current, entry.name);
    92	      if (entry.isSymbolicLink()) throw new Error(`release_manifest: symlink forbidden in package inventory: ${absPath}`);
    93	      if (entry.isDirectory()) walk(absPath);
    94	      else if (entry.isFile()) records.push(buildRecord(absPath, role, relPosix(absPath)));
    95	      else throw new Error(`release_manifest: unsupported package entry: ${absPath}`);
    96	    }
    97	  };
    98	  walk(dirAbsPath);
    99	  return records.sort((a, b) => a.path.localeCompare(b.path));
   100	}
   101	
   102	/** Enumerate *.mjs files directly inside dirAbsPath (no recursion), sorted. */
   103	function enumerateMjsDir(dirAbsPath, role) {
   104	  if (!fs.existsSync(dirAbsPath)) {
   105	    throw new Error(`release_manifest: expected directory missing: ${dirAbsPath}`);
   106	  }
   107	  return fs
   108	    .readdirSync(dirAbsPath, { withFileTypes: true })
   109	    .filter((entry) => entry.isFile() && entry.name.endsWith(".mjs"))
   110	    .map((entry) => buildRecord(path.join(dirAbsPath, entry.name), role))
   111	    .sort((a, b) => a.path.localeCompare(b.path));
   112	}
   113	
   114	/**
   115	 * Build the current manifest (array of records) by re-reading the real repo
   116	 * tree. Read-only — never mutates anything on disk.
   117	 */
   118	function buildCurrentRecords() {
   119	  const records = [
   120	    ...enumerateMjsDir(RUNTIME_LIB_DIR, "runtime_lib"),
   121	    ...enumerateTree(path.join(REPO_ROOT, "skills"), "plugin_skill"),
   122	    ...enumerateTree(path.join(REPO_ROOT, "hooks"), "plugin_hook"),
   123	    ...NAMED_PLUGIN_MANIFESTS.map((absPath) => {
   124	      if (!fs.existsSync(absPath)) {
   125	        throw new Error(`release_manifest: expected plugin manifest missing: ${absPath}`);
   126	      }
   127	      return buildRecord(absPath, "plugin_manifest", relPosix(absPath));
   128	    }),
   129	    buildRecord(PLUGIN_MARKETPLACE, "plugin_marketplace", relPosix(PLUGIN_MARKETPLACE)),
   130	    ...DISTRIBUTION_TOOLS.map((absPath) => buildRecord(absPath, "distribution_tool")),
   131	    ...DISTRIBUTION_TESTS.map((absPath) => buildRecord(absPath, "distribution_test")),
   132	  ];
   133	  records.sort((a, b) => a.path.localeCompare(b.path));
   134	  const duplicates = records.filter((record, index) => index > 0 && record.path === records[index - 1].path);
   135	  if (duplicates.length) throw new Error(`release_manifest: duplicate inventory path(s): ${duplicates.map((record) => record.path).join(", ")}`);
   136	  return records;
   137	}
   138	
   139	function writeManifestFile(records) {
   140	  const manifest = { schemaVersion: SCHEMA_VERSION, files: records };
   141	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(MANIFEST_PATH), { recursive: true });
   142	  fs.writeFileSync(MANIFEST_PATH, `${JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2)}\n`, "utf8");
   143	  return manifest;
   144	}
   145	
   146	function readStoredManifest() {
   147	  if (!fs.existsSync(MANIFEST_PATH)) {
   148	    throw new Error(`release_manifest: stored manifest missing: ${MANIFEST_PATH} (run --write first)`);
   149	  }
   150	  const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MANIFEST_PATH, "utf8"));
   151	  if (!parsed || !Array.isArray(parsed.files)) {
   152	    throw new Error(`release_manifest: stored manifest malformed: ${MANIFEST_PATH}`);
   153	  }
   154	  return parsed.files;
   155	}
   156	
   157	/**
   158	 * Compare two record arrays (stored vs current). Returns:
   159	 *   { ok, missing, added, changed }
   160	 * - missing: paths present in stored but not current
   161	 * - added: paths present in current but not stored
   162	 * - changed: [{ path, reasons: ["sha256"|"mode"|"role"] }] for paths in both
   163	 *   whose sha256/mode/role differ
   164	 * ok === true iff missing/added/changed are all empty.
   165	 */
   166	function compareManifests(stored, current) {
   167	  const storedByPath = new Map(stored.map((r) => [r.path, r]));
   168	  const currentByPath = new Map(current.map((r) => [r.path, r]));
   169	
   170	  const missing = [...storedByPath.keys()].filter((p) => !currentByPath.has(p)).sort();
   171	  const added = [...currentByPath.keys()].filter((p) => !storedByPath.has(p)).sort();
   172	
   173	  const changed = [];
   174	  for (const [p, storedRecord] of storedByPath) {
   175	    const currentRecord = currentByPath.get(p);
   176	    if (!currentRecord) continue;
   177	    const reasons = [];
   178	    if (storedRecord.sha256 !== currentRecord.sha256) reasons.push("sha256");
   179	    if (storedRecord.mode !== currentRecord.mode) reasons.push("mode");
   180	    if (storedRecord.role !== currentRecord.role) reasons.push("role");
   181	    if ((storedRecord.packagePath ?? null) !== (currentRecord.packagePath ?? null)) reasons.push("packagePath");
   182	    if (reasons.length > 0) changed.push({ path: p, reasons });
   183	  }
   184	  changed.sort((a, b) => a.path.localeCompare(b.path));
   185	
   186	  const ok = missing.length === 0 && added.length === 0 && changed.length === 0;
   187	  return { ok, missing, added, changed };
   188	}
   189	
   190	function printDiff(diffResult) {
   191	  for (const p of diffResult.missing) {
   192	    console.error(`MISMATCH missing (in stored manifest, absent from current tree): ${p}`);
   193	  }
   194	  for (const p of diffResult.added) {
   195	    console.error(`MISMATCH added (in current tree, absent from stored manifest): ${p}`);
   196	  }
   197	  for (const c of diffResult.changed) {
   198	    console.error(`MISMATCH ${c.path}: ${c.reasons.join(", ")} differ`);
   199	  }
   200	}
   201	
   202	function runWrite() {
   203	  const records = buildCurrentRecords();
   204	  writeManifestFile(records);
   205	  console.log(`WROTE ${relPosix(MANIFEST_PATH)}: ${records.length} file(s)`);
   206	  return 0;
   207	}
   208	
   209	function runCheck() {
   210	  const stored = readStoredManifest();
   211	  const current = buildCurrentRecords();
   212	  const diffResult = compareManifests(stored, current);
   213	  if (diffResult.ok) {
   214	    console.log(`release_manifest --check: ${current.length} file(s) match stored manifest`);
   215	    return 0;
   216	  }
   217	  printDiff(diffResult);
   218	  console.error(
   219	    `release_manifest --check: FAIL (${diffResult.missing.length} missing, ${diffResult.added.length} added, ${diffResult.changed.length} changed)`,
   220	  );
   221	  return 1;
   222	}
   223	
   224	function runSelftest() {
   225	  // Baseline: read the real, unmutated tree. Read-only.
   226	  const baseline = buildCurrentRecords();
   227	  if (baseline.length === 0) {
   228	    console.error("FAIL release_manifest --selftest: baseline manifest is empty, cannot prove anything");
   229	    return 1;
   230	  }

     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	// Guard: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/ and .bee/bin/lib/ must stay byte-
     3	// identical mirrors of each other. PROJ-08 (SPEC), crit-pattern 20260714
     4	// (derive file lists, never hand-enumerate): the template copy is what ships
     5	// to a fresh onboard, the .bee/bin copy is what actually runs in this repo —
     6	// if they drift, a fix lands in one and silently never reaches the other.
     7	
     8	import fs from "node:fs";
     9	import os from "node:os";
    10	import path from "node:path";
    11	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
    12	
    13	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    14	const REPO_ROOT = path.join(__dirname, "..");
    15	
    16	const TEMPLATES_LIB = path.join(REPO_ROOT, "skills", "bee-hive", "templates", "lib");
    17	const BIN_LIB = path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".bee", "bin", "lib");
    18	const SOURCE_HOOKS = path.join(REPO_ROOT, "hooks");
    19	const BIN_HOOKS = path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".bee", "bin", "hooks");
    20	const HOOK_PROJECTIONS = [path.join(SOURCE_HOOKS, "hooks.json"), path.join(SOURCE_HOOKS, "claude-hooks.json")];
    21	
    22	/**
    23	 * Lists the plain files (not directories) directly inside dir, derived via
    24	 * fs.readdirSync — never a hand-maintained list.
    25	 */
    26	function listFiles(dir) {
    27	  return fs
    28	    .readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })
    29	    .filter((entry) => entry.isFile())
    30	    .map((entry) => entry.name)
    31	    .sort();
    32	}
    33	
    34	/**
    35	 * Compares two directories of flat files: every file present in one side
    36	 * must be present and byte-identical in the other. Returns
    37	 * { ok, missingInB, extraInB, diffing } where:
    38	 *   - missingInB: files present in dirA but absent from dirB
    39	 *   - extraInB:   files present in dirB but absent from dirA
    40	 *   - diffing:    files present in both but with different byte content
    41	 */
    42	function compareDirs(dirA, dirB) {
    43	  const filesA = new Set(listFiles(dirA));
    44	  const filesB = new Set(listFiles(dirB));
    45	
    46	  const missingInB = [...filesA].filter((f) => !filesB.has(f)).sort();
    47	  const extraInB = [...filesB].filter((f) => !filesA.has(f)).sort();
    48	
    49	  const common = [...filesA].filter((f) => filesB.has(f)).sort();
    50	  const diffing = common.filter((file) => {
    51	    const bufA = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dirA, file));
    52	    const bufB = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dirB, file));
    53	    return !bufA.equals(bufB);
    54	  });
    55	
    56	  const ok = missingInB.length === 0 && extraInB.length === 0 && diffing.length === 0;
    57	  return { ok, missingInB, extraInB, diffing };
    58	}
    59	
    60	function launcherModules(projectionFiles) {
    61	  const out = new Set();
    62	  const visit = (value) => {
    63	    if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.forEach(visit);
    64	    if (!value || typeof value !== "object") return;
    65	    if (typeof value.command === "string") {
    66	      for (const match of value.command.matchAll(/(?:^|[\\/])(bee-[A-Za-z0-9-]+\.mjs)(?=["'\s]|$)/g)) {
    67	        out.add(match[1]);
    68	      }
    69	    }
    70	    Object.values(value).forEach(visit);
    71	  };
    72	  for (const file of projectionFiles) visit(JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(file, "utf8")));
    73	  return out;
    74	}
    75	
    76	function runtimeHookInventory(sourceDir, projectionFiles) {
    77	  const expected = launcherModules(projectionFiles);
    78	  const queue = [...expected];
    79	  const importRe = /(?:import|export)\s+(?:[^"']*?\s+from\s+)?["']\.\/([^"']+\.mjs)["']/g;
    80	  while (queue.length) {
    81	    const name = queue.shift();
    82	    const file = path.join(sourceDir, name);
    83	    if (!fs.existsSync(file)) throw new Error(`runtime hook inventory: launcher/import target missing: ${name}`);
    84	    const text = fs.readFileSync(file, "utf8");
    85	    for (const match of text.matchAll(importRe)) {
    86	      const dep = path.basename(match[1]);
    87	      if (dep !== match[1] || !dep.endsWith(".mjs")) {
    88	        throw new Error(`runtime hook inventory: unsafe relative import from ${name}: ${match[1]}`);
    89	      }
    90	      if (!expected.has(dep)) {
    91	        expected.add(dep);
    92	        queue.push(dep);
    93	      }
    94	    }
    95	  }
    96	  return [...expected].sort();
    97	}
    98	
    99	function compareInventory(expected, sourceDir, runtimeDir) {
   100	  const expectedSet = new Set(expected);
   101	  const actual = new Set(listFiles(runtimeDir));
   102	  const missing = expected.filter((name) => !actual.has(name));
   103	  const extra = [...actual].filter((name) => !expectedSet.has(name)).sort();
   104	  const diffing = expected
   105	    .filter((name) => actual.has(name))
   106	    .filter((name) => !fs.readFileSync(path.join(sourceDir, name)).equals(fs.readFileSync(path.join(runtimeDir, name))));
   107	  return { ok: missing.length === 0 && extra.length === 0 && diffing.length === 0, missing, extra, diffing };
   108	}
   109	
   110	// ─── internal self-test: prove the checker actually bites ─────────────────
   111	// Build two TEMP directories (never the real tree), seed them identically,
   112	// then inject a single byte-diff into one file on the copy side and assert
   113	// compareDirs() flags exactly that file. Everything is cleaned up after.
   114	{
   115	  const tmpRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "test-lib-mirror-selftest-"));
   116	  const dirA = path.join(tmpRoot, "a");
   117	  const dirB = path.join(tmpRoot, "b");
   118	  fs.mkdirSync(dirA);
   119	  fs.mkdirSync(dirB);
   120	
   121	  try {
   122	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirA, "same.mjs"), "export const x = 1;\n");
   123	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirB, "same.mjs"), "export const x = 1;\n");
   124	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirA, "diverges.mjs"), "export const y = 2;\n");
   125	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirB, "diverges.mjs"), "export const y = 3;\n"); // injected byte-diff
   126	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirA, "only-in-a.mjs"), "export const z = 4;\n");
   127	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(dirB, "only-in-b.mjs"), "export const w = 5;\n");
   128	
   129	    const selfTestResult = compareDirs(dirA, dirB);
   130	
   131	    const caughtDiff = selfTestResult.diffing.includes("diverges.mjs");
   132	    const caughtMissing = selfTestResult.missingInB.includes("only-in-a.mjs");
   133	    const caughtExtra = selfTestResult.extraInB.includes("only-in-b.mjs");
   134	
   135	    if (selfTestResult.ok || !caughtDiff || !caughtMissing || !caughtExtra) {
   136	      console.error("FAIL test_lib_mirror: internal self-test did not catch the injected byte-diff / missing / extra files");
   137	      console.error(`      checker result: ${JSON.stringify(selfTestResult)}`);
   138	      process.exit(1);
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    console.log("PASS test_lib_mirror: internal self-test — checker correctly flags injected byte-diff, missing file, and extra file");
   142	  } finally {
   143	    fs.rmSync(tmpRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
   144	  }
   145	}
   146	
   147	// Derivation self-test: launcher -> transitive same-directory import, plus
   148	// missing/extra/byte-drift detection. Source-only catalog/tests/config never
   149	// enter expected unless a production launcher imports them.
   150	{
   151	  const tmpRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "test-hook-mirror-selftest-"));
   152	  const source = path.join(tmpRoot, "source");
   153	  const runtime = path.join(tmpRoot, "runtime");
   154	  fs.mkdirSync(source);
   155	  fs.mkdirSync(runtime);
   156	  try {
   157	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(tmpRoot, "hooks.json"), JSON.stringify({ hooks: { Stop: [{ hooks: [{ command: 'node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/bee-a.mjs"' }] }] } }));
   158	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(source, "bee-a.mjs"), 'import "./adapter.mjs";\n');
   159	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(source, "adapter.mjs"), "export const x = 1;\n");
   160	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(source, "catalog.mjs"), "source only\n");
   161	    fs.copyFileSync(path.join(source, "bee-a.mjs"), path.join(runtime, "bee-a.mjs"));
   162	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(runtime, "adapter.mjs"), "export const x = 2;\n");
   163	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(runtime, "extra.mjs"), "extra\n");
   164	    const expected = runtimeHookInventory(source, [path.join(tmpRoot, "hooks.json")]);
   165	    const injected = compareInventory(expected, source, runtime);
   166	    if (
   167	      expected.join(",") !== "adapter.mjs,bee-a.mjs" ||
   168	      !injected.diffing.includes("adapter.mjs") ||
   169	      !injected.extra.includes("extra.mjs") ||
   170	      injected.missing.length !== 0
   171	    ) {
   172	      throw new Error(`hook inventory self-test did not catch derivation/diff/extra: ${JSON.stringify({ expected, injected })}`);
   173	    }
   174	    fs.rmSync(path.join(runtime, "bee-a.mjs"));
   175	    const missing = compareInventory(expected, source, runtime);
   176	    if (!missing.missing.includes("bee-a.mjs")) {
   177	      throw new Error(`hook inventory self-test did not catch missing launcher: ${JSON.stringify(missing)}`);
   178	    }
   179	    console.log("PASS test_lib_mirror: runtime hook inventory derives launchers/imports and catches missing, extra, and byte drift");
   180	  } finally {
   181	    fs.rmSync(tmpRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
   182	  }
   183	}
   184	
   185	// ─── real check: THIS repo's real templates/lib <-> .bee/bin/lib mirror ────
   186	
   187	if (!fs.existsSync(TEMPLATES_LIB)) {
   188	  console.error(`FAIL test_lib_mirror: ${TEMPLATES_LIB} does not exist`);
   189	  process.exit(1);
   190	}
   191	if (!fs.existsSync(BIN_LIB)) {
   192	  console.error(`FAIL test_lib_mirror: ${BIN_LIB} does not exist`);
   193	  process.exit(1);
   194	}
   195	
   196	const result = compareDirs(TEMPLATES_LIB, BIN_LIB);
   197	if (!result.ok) {
   198	  console.error("FAIL test_lib_mirror: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/ and .bee/bin/lib/ have drifted:");
   199	  if (result.missingInB.length) {
   200	    console.error(`      present in templates/lib but missing from .bee/bin/lib: ${result.missingInB.join(", ")}`);
   201	  }
   202	  if (result.extraInB.length) {
   203	    console.error(`      present in .bee/bin/lib but missing from templates/lib: ${result.extraInB.join(", ")}`);
   204	  }
   205	  if (result.diffing.length) {
   206	    console.error(`      byte-diffing in both: ${result.diffing.join(", ")}`);
   207	  }
   208	  process.exit(1);
   209	}
   210	
   211	console.log(`PASS test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (${listFiles(TEMPLATES_LIB).length} files)`);
   212	
   213	if (!fs.existsSync(BIN_HOOKS)) {
   214	  console.error(`FAIL test_lib_mirror: ${BIN_HOOKS} does not exist`);
   215	  process.exit(1);
   216	}
   217	const hookExpected = runtimeHookInventory(SOURCE_HOOKS, HOOK_PROJECTIONS);
   218	const hookResult = compareInventory(hookExpected, SOURCE_HOOKS, BIN_HOOKS);
   219	if (!hookResult.ok) {
   220	  console.error(`FAIL test_lib_mirror: runtime-derived hook mirror drifted: ${JSON.stringify(hookResult)}`);
   221	  process.exit(1);
   222	}
   223	console.log(`PASS test_lib_mirror: runtime-derived hook inventory is byte-identical (${hookExpected.length} files)`);

codex
One high-risk edge is now clear: HTML comments hide only the marker text, not the Markdown between them. Any plugin/global route that still copies canonical `skills/` verbatim would load both runtime branches after tagging. I’m checking every distribution target now, because this determines whether the slice needs a planning correction rather than a test-only adjustment.

   430	
   431	# Immediate up_to_date recheck: a fresh onboarding plan must find nothing to do.
   432	# This proves onboarding/runtime/project-projection surfaces all equal the source
   433	# tuple (any drift would re-plan work here).
   434	RECHECK="$(node "$ONBOARD" --repo-root "$TARGET_DIR" --json ${ONBOARD_FLAGS[@]+"${ONBOARD_FLAGS[@]}"} 2>/dev/null)" \
   435	  || fail "Verification failed: onboarding recheck did not run."
   436	printf '%s' "$RECHECK" | node -e '
   437	  const s = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0, "utf8"));
   438	  if (s.status !== "up_to_date") {
   439	    console.error(`onboarding recheck expected up_to_date, got ${s.status}`);
   440	    process.exit(1);
   441	  }
   442	' || fail "Verification failed: onboarding is not up_to_date immediately after apply."
   443	
   444	# Plugin-first: the distribution recheck must also report nothing left to clean.
   445	if [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ]; then
   446	  probe_plugin_state "$STATE_FILE"
   447	  node "$DIST_HELPER" "${DIST_ARGS[@]}" >/dev/null || fail "Verification failed: distribution recheck refused."
   448	fi
   449	
   450	log ""
   451	log "bee installed."
   452	log "  next: open an agent session in $TARGET_DIR"
   453	log "  - Claude Code: the session preamble appears via hooks; or say \"Route this through bee: <task>\""
   454	log "  - Codex: the AGENTS.md BEE block bootstraps; first step is bee.mjs status"
