{
  "name": "bee",
  "version": "1.5.0",
  "description": "bee: validate-first agentic development skills — staged workflow with four human gates, evidence-based validation, bounded swarms over verified cells, and a compounding knowledge loop.",
  "author": {
    "name": "bee"
  },
  "license": "MIT",
  "keywords": [
    "bee",
    "skills",
    "agentic-development",
    "planning",
    "multi-agent",
    "validate-first"
  ],
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "Bee",
    "shortDescription": "Validate-first skills for agentic software delivery",
    "longDescription": "Bundles the bee workflow skills: hive bootstrap and routing, exploring, planning, validating, swarming, executing, reviewing, scribing (BA-grade tech-agnostic area specs), compounding, grooming, and skill authoring. Hard gates, verified cells, bounded workers, compounding learnings.",
    "developerName": "bee",
    "category": "Productivity",
    "capabilities": [
      "Plan",
      "Write",
      "Review",
      "Learn"
    ],
    "defaultPrompt": [
      "Onboard this repository for bee and start with bee-hive.",
      "Route a feature request through the full bee chain.",
      "Plan and validate a feature before we write code."
    ],
    "brandColor": "#B45309"
  }
}
{
  "name": "bee",
  "version": "1.5.0",
  "description": "bee: validate-first agentic development for Claude Code and Codex — staged workflow with four human gates, evidence-based validation, bounded swarms over verified cells, and a compounding knowledge loop.",
  "author": {
    "name": "bee"
  },
  "hooks": "./hooks/claude-hooks.json"
}
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	import fs from "node:fs";
     4	import path from "node:path";
     5	import { createHash, randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
     6	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
     7	
     8	const PROJECT_SKILL_ROOTS = [".claude/skills", ".agents/skills", ".codex/skills"];
     9	const PROJECT_HOOK_FILES = [".claude/settings.json", ".codex/hooks.json"];
    10	const PACKAGE_ROLES = new Set(["plugin_skill", "plugin_hook", "plugin_manifest", "plugin_marketplace"]);
    11	const BEE_HOOK_HANDLERS = new Set([
    12	  "bee-session-init.mjs", "bee-prompt-context.mjs", "bee-write-guard.mjs",
    13	  "bee-model-guard.mjs", "bee-state-sync.mjs", "bee-chain-nudge.mjs",
    14	  "bee-session-close.mjs", "bee-codex-subagent-audit.mjs", "bee-tools-logger.mjs",
    15	]);
    16	
    17	function sha256(data) {
    18	  return createHash("sha256").update(data).digest("hex");
    19	}
    20	
    21	function fail(message) {
    22	  const error = new Error(message);
    23	  error.code = "DISTRIBUTION_REFUSED";
    24	  throw error;
    25	}
    26	
    27	function isInside(root, candidate) {
    28	  const relative = path.relative(root, candidate);
    29	  return relative === "" || (!relative.startsWith(`..${path.sep}`) && relative !== "..");
    30	}
    31	
    32	function lstatOrNull(target) {
    33	  try { return fs.lstatSync(target); } catch (error) {
    34	    if (error.code === "ENOENT") return null;
    35	    throw error;
    36	  }
    37	}
    38	
    39	function assertPlainDirectory(target, label, { allowMissing = false } = {}) {
    40	  const stat = lstatOrNull(target);
    41	  if (!stat && allowMissing) return null;
    42	  if (!stat) fail(`${label} is missing: ${target}`);
    43	  if (stat.isSymbolicLink() || !stat.isDirectory()) fail(`${label} must be a plain directory: ${target}`);
    44	  const resolved = fs.realpathSync.native(target);
    45	  if (resolved !== path.resolve(target)) fail(`${label} aliases another path: ${target}`);
    46	  return stat;
    47	}
    48	
    49	function assertPlainFile(target, label) {
    50	  const stat = lstatOrNull(target);
    51	  if (!stat || stat.isSymbolicLink() || !stat.isFile()) fail(`${label} must be a plain file: ${target}`);
    52	  return stat;
    53	}
    54	
    55	function walkPlainFiles(root, relative = "") {
    56	  const current = path.join(root, relative);
    57	  const stat = lstatOrNull(current);
    58	  if (!stat) return [];
    59	  if (stat.isSymbolicLink()) fail(`symlink is forbidden in inventory: ${current}`);
    60	  if (stat.isFile()) return [{ path: relative.split(path.sep).join("/"), abs: current, stat }];
    61	  if (!stat.isDirectory()) fail(`unsupported inventory entry: ${current}`);
    62	  const result = [];
    63	  for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true }).sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name))) {
    64	    const childRel = path.join(relative, entry.name);
    65	    if (entry.isSymbolicLink()) fail(`symlink is forbidden in inventory: ${path.join(root, childRel)}`);
    66	    result.push(...walkPlainFiles(root, childRel));
    67	  }
    68	  return result;
    69	}
    70	
    71	function fileRecord(abs, rel) {
    72	  const stat = assertPlainFile(abs, "inventory entry");
    73	  return {
    74	    path: rel.split(path.sep).join("/"),
    75	    sha256: sha256(fs.readFileSync(abs)),
    76	    mode: (stat.mode & 0o777).toString(8).padStart(3, "0"),
    77	  };
    78	}
    79	
    80	export function loadPackageInventory(manifestPath) {
    81	  assertPlainFile(manifestPath, "release manifest");
    82	  let manifest;
    83	  try { manifest = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, "utf8")); } catch (error) {
    84	    fail(`release manifest is not valid JSON: ${error.message}`);
    85	  }
    86	  if (!manifest || !Array.isArray(manifest.files)) fail("release manifest has no files array");
    87	  const inventory = manifest.files
    88	    .filter((record) => PACKAGE_ROLES.has(record.role))
    89	    .map((record) => ({
    90	      path: record.packagePath ?? record.path,
    91	      sha256: record.sha256,
    92	      mode: record.mode,
    93	      role: record.role,
    94	    }))
    95	    .sort((a, b) => a.path.localeCompare(b.path));
    96	  if (inventory.length === 0) fail("release manifest has no package inventory records");
    97	  const seen = new Set();
    98	  for (const record of inventory) {
    99	    if (!record.path || path.isAbsolute(record.path) || record.path.split(/[\\/]/).includes("..")) fail(`unsafe package path: ${record.path}`);
   100	    if (!/^[a-f0-9]{64}$/.test(record.sha256) || !/^[0-7]{3}$/.test(record.mode)) fail(`malformed package record: ${record.path}`);
   101	    if (seen.has(record.path)) fail(`duplicate package record: ${record.path}`);
   102	    seen.add(record.path);
   103	  }
   104	  return inventory;
   105	}
   106	
   107	export function managedSkillNames(inventory) {
   108	  if (!Array.isArray(inventory)) fail("release inventory is not an array");
   109	  const names = new Set();
   110	  for (const record of inventory) {
   111	    if (record?.role !== "plugin_skill") continue;
   112	    if (typeof record.path !== "string") fail(`release inventory has a malformed plugin skill record`);
   113	    const segments = record.path.split("/");
   114	    if (segments[0] !== "skills" || !segments[1]) fail(`release inventory has an unexpected plugin skill path: ${record.path}`);
   115	    if (!/^bee-[a-z0-9-]+$/.test(segments[1])) fail(`release inventory names an unsafe managed skill: ${segments[1]}`);
   116	    names.add(segments[1]);
   117	  }
   118	  if (names.size === 0) fail("release inventory names no managed plugin skills");
   119	  return names;
   120	}
   121	
   122	function normalizePluginList(payload) {
   123	  if (Array.isArray(payload)) return payload;
   124	  for (const key of ["plugins", "items", "data"]) if (Array.isArray(payload?.[key])) return payload[key];
   125	  return payload && typeof payload === "object" ? [payload] : [];
   126	}
   127	
   128	export function discoverBeePlugin(payload, runtime) {
   129	  const candidates = normalizePluginList(payload);
   130	  const plugin = candidates.find((item) => {
   131	    const name = item?.name ?? item?.id ?? item?.plugin?.name;
   132	    return name === "bee" || String(name ?? "").startsWith("bee@");
   133	  });
   134	  if (!plugin) return { runtime, installed: false, enabled: false, root: null, version: null };
   135	  const state = String(plugin.status ?? plugin.state ?? "").toLowerCase();
   136	  const installed = plugin.installed === true || !["removed", "not_installed"].includes(state);
   137	  const enabled = installed && (plugin.enabled === true || ["enabled", "active"].includes(state));
   138	  return {
   139	    runtime,
   140	    installed,
   141	    enabled,
   142	    root: plugin.root ?? plugin.path ?? plugin.installPath ?? plugin.install_path ?? plugin.sourcePath ?? plugin.source_path ?? null,
   143	    version: plugin.version ?? plugin.plugin?.version ?? null,
   144	    sourceKind: plugin.sourceKind ?? plugin.source_kind ?? plugin.provenance ?? null,
   145	  };
   146	}
   147	
   148	export function proveInstalledPackage(state, expectedInventory) {
   149	  if (!state?.installed || !state?.enabled) fail(`${state?.runtime ?? "runtime"} bee plugin is not installed and enabled`);
   150	  if (["source_checkout", "checkout", "repository"].includes(String(state.sourceKind ?? "").toLowerCase())) fail("source checkout cannot substitute for an installed plugin package");
   151	  if (typeof state.root !== "string" || !path.isAbsolute(state.root)) fail("enabled plugin did not report an absolute installed package root");
   152	  const packageRoot = path.resolve(state.root);
   153	  assertPlainDirectory(packageRoot, "installed package root");
   154	  const expected = new Map(expectedInventory.map((record) => [record.path, record]));
   155	  const prefixes = [...new Set(expectedInventory.map((record) => record.path.split("/")[0]))];
   156	  const actual = new Map();
   157	  for (const prefix of prefixes) {
   158	    const prefixPath = path.join(packageRoot, prefix);
   159	    const stat = lstatOrNull(prefixPath);
   160	    if (!stat) continue;
   161	    if (stat.isSymbolicLink()) fail(`installed package prefix is a symlink: ${prefix}`);
   162	    if (stat.isFile()) actual.set(prefix, fileRecord(prefixPath, prefix));
   163	    else for (const item of walkPlainFiles(prefixPath)) {
   164	      const rel = [prefix, item.path].filter(Boolean).join("/");
   165	      actual.set(rel, fileRecord(item.abs, rel));
   166	    }
   167	  }
   168	  const missing = [...expected.keys()].filter((key) => !actual.has(key));
   169	  const unexpected = [...actual.keys()].filter((key) => !expected.has(key));
   170	  const changed = [...expected.entries()].filter(([key, record]) => {
   171	    const value = actual.get(key);
   172	    return value && (value.sha256 !== record.sha256 || value.mode !== record.mode);
   173	  }).map(([key]) => key);
   174	  if (missing.length || unexpected.length || changed.length) {
   175	    fail(`installed package inventory mismatch (missing=${missing.join(",") || "none"}; unexpected=${unexpected.join(",") || "none"}; changed=${changed.join(",") || "none"})`);
   176	  }
   177	  return { root: packageRoot, files: actual.size, version: state.version };
   178	}
   179	
   180	export function provePluginInactive(states) {
   181	  const active = states.filter((state) => state.installed || state.enabled);
   182	  if (active.length) fail(`bee plugin remains active for: ${active.map((state) => state.runtime).join(", ")}`);
   183	  return { inactive: states.map((state) => state.runtime) };
   184	}
   185	
   186	function recognizedBeeCommand(command) {
   187	  if (typeof command !== "string") return false;
   188	  const normalized = command.replaceAll("\\", "/");
   189	  const handler = [...BEE_HOOK_HANDLERS].find((name) => normalized.includes(`/hooks/${name}`));
   190	  if (!handler) return false;
   191	  return normalized.includes("CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT") || normalized.includes("CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR") || normalized.includes("/.bee/bin/hooks/") || normalized.includes("/hooks/bee-");
   192	}
   193	
   194	function cleanHookConfig(absPath) {
   195	  const stat = lstatOrNull(absPath);
   196	  if (!stat) return null;
   197	  assertPlainFile(absPath, "hook configuration");
   198	  let json;
   199	  try { json = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(absPath, "utf8")); } catch (error) { fail(`malformed hook configuration ${absPath}: ${error.message}`); }
   200	  if (!json || typeof json !== "object" || Array.isArray(json)) fail(`hook configuration must be an object: ${absPath}`);
   201	  if (json.hooks === undefined) return null;
   202	  if (!json.hooks || typeof json.hooks !== "object" || Array.isArray(json.hooks)) fail(`hooks must be an object: ${absPath}`);
   203	  let removed = 0;
   204	  const next = structuredClone(json);
   205	  for (const [event, groups] of Object.entries(next.hooks)) {
   206	    if (!Array.isArray(groups)) fail(`hook event ${event} must be an array: ${absPath}`);
   207	    next.hooks[event] = groups.filter((group) => {
   208	      if (!group || typeof group !== "object" || Array.isArray(group) || !Array.isArray(group.hooks)) fail(`hook group ${event} is malformed: ${absPath}`);
   209	      const kept = group.hooks.filter((hook) => {
   210	        if (!hook || typeof hook !== "object" || Array.isArray(hook)) fail(`hook entry ${event} is malformed: ${absPath}`);

exec
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba scripts/install.sh | sed -n '170,390p'; nl -ba scripts/install.ps1 | sed -n '180,470p'" in /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog
 succeeded in 199ms:

   176	[ -f "$ONBOARD" ] || fail "Not a bee checkout (missing skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs): $BEE_SRC"

   178	RELEASE_MANIFEST="$BEE_SRC/docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json"
   179	[ -f "$DIST_HELPER" ] || fail "Not a bee release (missing plugin_distribution.mjs): $BEE_SRC"

   181	BEE_VERSION="$(node -e "console.log(JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8')).version)" "$BEE_SRC/.claude-plugin/plugin.json" 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
   182	log "source   $BEE_SRC (bee $BEE_VERSION)"
   183	
   184	# Direct global replacement is intentionally gone: user-root cleanup is legal
   185	# only through an exact ownership ledger consumed by the shared planner.
   186	if [ "$GLOBAL_SKILLS" -eq 1 ] && [ -z "$OWNERSHIP_LEDGER" ]; then
   187	  fail "--global-skills requires --ownership-ledger; basename-only global replacement is refused"
   188	fi
   189	
   190	# ---------- layer 2: target repo (greenfield / brownfield) ----------
   191	
   192	if [ ! -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]; then
   193	  if [ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]; then
   194	    log "would create  $TARGET_DIR (greenfield)"
   195	  else
   196	    confirm "Target $TARGET_DIR does not exist. Create it (greenfield)?" || fail "Aborted."
   197	    mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR" 2>/dev/null \
   198	      || fail "cannot create target directory '$TARGET_DIR' (permission denied or invalid path). Pass a real, writable path to -d — e.g. -d ~/projects/my-app — not a literal '/path/to/...' placeholder."
   199	  fi
   200	fi
   201	TARGET_DIR="$(cd "$TARGET_DIR" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P || printf '%s' "$TARGET_DIR")"
   202	
   203	MODE="brownfield"
   204	if [ ! -e "$TARGET_DIR/.git" ]; then
   205	  MODE="greenfield"
   206	  if [ "$GIT_INIT" -eq 1 ]; then
   207	    if [ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]; then
   208	      log "would run     git init ($TARGET_DIR is not a git repo)"
   209	    elif command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && confirm "No git repo at $TARGET_DIR. Run git init?"; then
   210	      git -C "$TARGET_DIR" init --quiet
   211	    fi
   212	  fi
   213	elif [ -f "$TARGET_DIR/.bee/onboarding.json" ]; then
   214	  MODE="brownfield (bee already onboarded — refresh)"
   215	elif [ -f "$TARGET_DIR/AGENTS.md" ] || [ -f "$TARGET_DIR/CLAUDE.md" ]; then
   216	  MODE="brownfield (existing agent docs — BEE block will be merged, nothing outside markers touched)"
   217	fi
   218	log "target   $TARGET_DIR [$MODE]"
   219	
   220	ONBOARD_FLAGS=()
   221	if [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ]; then
   222	  ONBOARD_FLAGS+=("--plugin-source")
   223	elif [ "$REPO_HOOKS" -eq 1 ]; then
   224	  ONBOARD_FLAGS+=("--repo-hooks")
   225	fi
   226	if [ "$NO_CLAUDE_MD" -eq 1 ]; then
   227	  ONBOARD_FLAGS+=("--no-claude-md")
   228	fi
   229	if [ "$GLOBAL_SKILLS" -eq 1 ]; then
   230	  ONBOARD_FLAGS+=("--global-skills")
   231	fi
   232	
   233	runtime_active() { case "$RUNTIME" in "$1"|both) return 0 ;; *) return 1 ;; esac; }
   234	
   235	# D8: pre-confirmation is READ-ONLY. probe_plugin_state runs only `plugin list`
   236	# status probes (never install/remove/marketplace), records the current runtime
   237	# plugin state into $1, and never mutates a runtime plugin, target, or home.
   238	probe_plugin_state() {
   239	  local dest="$1"
   240	  if [ -n "$PLUGIN_STATE_FILE" ]; then
   241	    [ -f "$PLUGIN_STATE_FILE" ] || fail "--plugin-state-file not found: $PLUGIN_STATE_FILE"
   242	    STATE_FILE="$PLUGIN_STATE_FILE"
   243	    return
   244	  fi
   245	  local claude_json="$STATE_TMP/claude.json" codex_json="$STATE_TMP/codex.json"
   246	  printf '[]\n' > "$claude_json"; printf '[]\n' > "$codex_json"
   247	  if runtime_active codex; then
   248	    if command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   249	      codex plugin list --json > "$codex_json" || fail "Codex plugin status probe failed"
   250	    elif [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ]; then fail "Codex CLI is required for plugin-first"; fi
   251	  fi
   252	  if runtime_active claude; then
   253	    if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   254	      claude plugin list --json > "$claude_json" || fail "Claude plugin status probe failed"
   255	    elif [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ]; then fail "Claude CLI is required for plugin-first"; fi
   256	  fi
   257	  node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const read=p=>JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,"utf8")); fs.writeFileSync(process.argv[3], JSON.stringify({claude:read(process.argv[1]),codex:read(process.argv[2])}));' "$claude_json" "$codex_json" "$dest" \
   258	    || fail "Plugin status probe returned unreadable data (package-list shape drift)"
   259	}
   260	
   261	# Whether the bee plugin was installed for <runtime> in the pre-run snapshot.
   262	# Prints 1/0; used to decide the inverse transition during rollback.
   263	plugin_was_installed() {
   264	  local rt="$1" src="$2"
   265	  node -e '
   266	    const fs=require("fs");
   267	    const s=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],"utf8"));
   268	    const list=s[process.argv[2]];
   269	    const arr=Array.isArray(list)?list:((list&&(list.plugins||list.items||list.data))||(list&&typeof list==="object"?[list]:[]));
   270	    const p=arr.find(x=>{const n=x&&(x.name||x.id||(x.plugin&&x.plugin.name));return n==="bee"||String(n||"").startsWith("bee@")});
   271	    let out="0";
   272	    if(p){const st=String(p.status||p.state||"").toLowerCase();out=(p.installed===true||!["removed","not_installed"].includes(st))?"1":"0";}
   273	    process.stdout.write(out);
   274	  ' "$src" "$rt" 2>/dev/null || printf '0'
   275	}
   276	
   277	# POST-confirmation transition: plugin-first installs the plugin package; repo-copy
   278	# removes it. Returns nonzero if a required plugin-first transition fails.
   279	transition_plugin() {
   280	  [ -n "$PLUGIN_STATE_FILE" ] && return 0
   281	  local rt add_verb rm_verb
   282	  for rt in codex claude; do
   283	    runtime_active "$rt" || continue
   284	    command -v "$rt" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ] && fail "$rt CLI is required for plugin-first"; continue; }
   285	    if [ "$rt" = "codex" ]; then add_verb="add"; rm_verb="remove"; else add_verb="install"; rm_verb="uninstall"; fi
   286	    if [ "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" = "plugin-first" ]; then
   287	      # Mutation verbs take NO --json (only `plugin list` does); the real CLIs
   288	      # reject `--json` here with `error: unknown option '--json'`.
   289	      "$rt" plugin marketplace add "$BEE_SRC" >/dev/null || return 1
   290	      "$rt" plugin "$add_verb" bee@bee >/dev/null || return 1
   291	    else
   292	      "$rt" plugin "$rm_verb" bee@bee >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   293	    fi
   294	  done
   295	  return 0
   296	}
   297	
   298	# Restore every runtime to its exact pre-run installed/enabled state. Rollback
   299	# is HONEST: it re-probes the CURRENT state and only acts where it genuinely
   300	# differs from the pre-run snapshot. If the failing transition never actually
   301	# installed or removed anything (e.g. it died at `marketplace add`), current ==
   302	# pre-run for every runtime and rollback is a no-op SUCCESS — it must not try to
   303	# remove a never-installed plugin and misreport that remove-of-absent as a
   304	# failed rollback. Returns nonzero only when restoring a genuinely changed
   305	# runtime back to its pre-run state fails.
   306	rollback_plugin() {
   307	  [ -n "$PLUGIN_STATE_FILE" ] && return 0
   308	  local rc=0 rt was now add_verb rm_verb
   309	  local now_state="$STATE_TMP/rollback-state.json"
   310	  probe_plugin_state "$now_state"
   311	  for rt in codex claude; do
   312	    runtime_active "$rt" || continue
   313	    command -v "$rt" >/dev/null 2>&1 || continue
   314	    if [ "$rt" = "codex" ]; then add_verb="add"; rm_verb="remove"; else add_verb="install"; rm_verb="uninstall"; fi
   315	    was="$(plugin_was_installed "$rt" "$PRE_STATE_FILE")"
   316	    now="$(plugin_was_installed "$rt" "$now_state")"
   317	    [ "$was" = "$now" ] && continue   # already at the pre-run state: nothing to restore
   318	    if [ "$was" = "1" ]; then
   319	      # pre-run had the plugin, the transition removed it: re-install.
   320	      "$rt" plugin marketplace add "$BEE_SRC" >/dev/null 2>&1 || rc=1
   321	      "$rt" plugin "$add_verb" bee@bee >/dev/null 2>&1 || rc=1
   322	    else
   323	      # pre-run lacked the plugin, the transition installed it: remove it.
   324	      "$rt" plugin "$rm_verb" bee@bee >/dev/null 2>&1 || rc=1
   325	    fi
   326	  done
   327	  return $rc
   328	}
   329	
   330	# A post-transition failure: roll the plugin state back to the pre-run snapshot,
   331	# leave the target untouched, report BOTH the primary and any rollback failure,
   332	# and exit nonzero (never convert a failed install into success).
   333	handle_transition_failure() {
   334	  printf 'Error: %s\n' "$1" >&2
   335	  if rollback_plugin; then
   336	    printf 'rollback: pre-run plugin state restored; target left unchanged\n' >&2
   337	  else
   338	    printf 'Error: rollback failed to fully restore the pre-run plugin state\n' >&2
   339	  fi
   340	  exit 1
   341	}
   342	
   343	STATE_TMP="$(mktemp -d)"
   344	STATE_FILE="$STATE_TMP/state.json"
   345	PRE_STATE_FILE="$STATE_TMP/pre-state.json"
   346	
   347	# 1. read-only probe of the CURRENT plugin state (pre-confirmation, no mutation).
   348	probe_plugin_state "$STATE_FILE"
   349	cp "$STATE_FILE" "$PRE_STATE_FILE"
   350	
   351	DIST_ARGS=(--mode "$DISTRIBUTION_MODE" --runtime "$RUNTIME" --repo-root "$TARGET_DIR" --release-manifest "$RELEASE_MANIFEST" --plugin-state-file "$STATE_FILE")
   352	if [ -n "$OWNERSHIP_LEDGER" ]; then DIST_ARGS+=(--ledger "$OWNERSHIP_LEDGER"); fi
   353	if [ "$GLOBAL_SKILLS" -eq 1 ]; then
   354	  if [ "$RUNTIME" = "claude" ] || [ "$RUNTIME" = "both" ]; then DIST_ARGS+=(--user-skill-root "${CLAUDE_HOME:-$HOME/.claude}/skills"); fi
   355	  if [ "$RUNTIME" = "codex" ] || [ "$RUNTIME" = "both" ]; then DIST_ARGS+=(--user-skill-root "${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/skills"); fi
   356	fi
   357	
   358	# onboard_plan_json prints the onboarding plan as JSON (plan mode, writes nothing).
   359	onboard_plan_json() {
   360	  node "$ONBOARD" --repo-root "$TARGET_DIR" --json ${ONBOARD_FLAGS[@]+"${ONBOARD_FLAGS[@]}"} 2>/dev/null
   361	}
   362	# plan_field <json> <field> — extract one string field, or "parse_error" on bad JSON.
   363	plan_field() {
   364	  printf '%s' "$1" | node -e 'let d="";process.stdin.on("data",c=>{d+=c}).on("end",()=>{try{const s=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(String(s[process.argv[1]]??""));}catch{process.stdout.write("parse_error");}})' "$2"
   365	}
   366	
   367	# 2. mutation-free preview: onboarding plan (writes nothing). A blocked/refused
   368	#    plan (invalid or mixed source tuple, refused downgrade) must fail loudly HERE,
   369	#    before any confirmation, transition, or target/home write. onboard_bee reports
   370	#    a refusal as a non-`changes_needed`/`up_to_date` status (and may still exit 0),
   371	#    so status — not exit code alone — is the gate.
   372	log "plan     onboard_bee.mjs ${ONBOARD_FLAGS[*]:-} (preview, writes nothing)"
   373	PREVIEW_JSON="$(onboard_plan_json)" || fail "Onboarding plan failed."
   374	PREVIEW_STATUS="$(plan_field "$PREVIEW_JSON" status)"
   375	case "$PREVIEW_STATUS" in
   376	  up_to_date|changes_needed) log "plan     status: $PREVIEW_STATUS" ;;
   377	  *) fail "Onboarding refused before any change [$PREVIEW_STATUS]: $(plan_field "$PREVIEW_JSON" reason)" ;;
   378	esac
   379	
   380	if [ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]; then
   381	  log "dry-run  nothing written, no plugin changes. Re-run without --dry-run to apply."
   382	  exit 0
   383	fi
   384	
   385	# 3. confirmation gate. Nothing above this line mutates a plugin, target, or home.
   386	confirm "Apply this onboarding plan to $TARGET_DIR?" || fail "Aborted — nothing applied."
   387	
   388	# 4. transition the selected plugin, then re-probe and revalidate before onboarding.
   389	transition_plugin || handle_transition_failure "Plugin transition failed"
   390	
   180	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $stateTempDir | Out-Null
   181	    $claudeStatePath = Join-Path $stateTempDir 'claude.json'
   182	    $codexStatePath = Join-Path $stateTempDir 'codex.json'
   183	    Set-Content -Encoding ASCII -Path $claudeStatePath -Value '[]'
   184	    Set-Content -Encoding ASCII -Path $codexStatePath -Value '[]'
   185	
   186	    if ($Runtime -in @('codex', 'both')) {
   187	      $codex = Get-Command codex -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   188	      if ($codex) {
   189	        if (-not $DryRun) {
   190	          if ($Distribution -eq 'plugin-first') {
   191	            # Mutation verbs take NO --json (only `plugin list` does); the real
   192	            # CLI rejects `--json` here with `error: unknown option '--json'`.
   193	            & codex plugin marketplace add $beeSrc | Out-Null
   194	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Codex marketplace registration failed' }
   195	            & codex plugin add 'bee@bee' | Out-Null
   196	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Codex bee plugin install failed' }
   197	          } else {
   198	            & codex plugin remove 'bee@bee' | Out-Null
   199	          }
   200	        }
   201	        $codexList = & codex plugin list --json
   202	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Codex plugin status probe failed' }
   203	        Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 -Path $codexStatePath -Value ($codexList -join "`n")
   204	      } elseif ($Distribution -eq 'plugin-first') { Fail 'Codex CLI is required for plugin-first' }
   205	    }
   206	
   207	    if ($Runtime -in @('claude', 'both')) {
   208	      $claude = Get-Command claude -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   209	      if ($claude) {
   210	        if (-not $DryRun) {
   211	          if ($Distribution -eq 'plugin-first') {
   212	            & claude plugin marketplace add $beeSrc | Out-Null
   213	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Claude marketplace registration failed' }
   214	            & claude plugin install 'bee@bee' | Out-Null
   215	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Claude bee plugin install failed' }
   216	          } else {
   217	            & claude plugin uninstall 'bee@bee' | Out-Null
   218	          }
   219	        }
   220	        $claudeList = & claude plugin list --json
   221	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Claude plugin status probe failed' }
   222	        Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 -Path $claudeStatePath -Value ($claudeList -join "`n")
   223	      } elseif ($Distribution -eq 'plugin-first') { Fail 'Claude CLI is required for plugin-first' }
   224	    }
   225	
   226	    $combined = @{ claude = (Get-Content $claudeStatePath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json); codex = (Get-Content $codexStatePath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json) }
   227	    $stateFile = Join-Path $stateTempDir 'state.json'
   228	    Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 -Path $stateFile -Value ($combined | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100)
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  $distributionArgs = @('--mode', $Distribution, '--runtime', $Runtime, '--repo-root', $Directory, '--release-manifest', $releaseManifest, '--plugin-state-file', $stateFile)
   232	  if ($OwnershipLedger) { $distributionArgs += @('--ledger', $OwnershipLedger) }
   233	  if ($GlobalSkills) {
   234	    $claudeHome = if ($env:CLAUDE_HOME) { $env:CLAUDE_HOME } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }
   235	    $codexHome = if ($env:CODEX_HOME) { $env:CODEX_HOME } else { Join-Path $HOME '.codex' }
   236	    if ($Runtime -in @('claude', 'both')) { $distributionArgs += @('--user-skill-root', (Join-Path $claudeHome 'skills')) }
   237	    if ($Runtime -in @('codex', 'both')) { $distributionArgs += @('--user-skill-root', (Join-Path $codexHome 'skills')) }
   238	  }
   239	
   240	  node $distributionHelper @distributionArgs
   241	  if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Distribution preflight refused' }
   242	
   243	  Write-Host "plan     onboard_bee.mjs $($onboardFlags -join ' ') (dry-run first)"
   244	  node $onboard --repo-root $Directory @onboardFlags
   245	  if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Onboarding plan failed.' }
   246	
   247	  if ($DryRun) {
   248	    Write-Host 'dry-run  nothing written. Re-run without -DryRun to apply.'
   249	    exit 0
   250	  }
   251	
   252	  if (-not (Confirm-Step "Apply this onboarding plan to $Directory?")) { Fail 'Aborted - nothing applied.' }
   253	  node $onboard --repo-root $Directory --apply @onboardFlags | Out-Null
   254	  if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Onboarding apply failed.' }
   255	
   256	  if ($Distribution -eq 'plugin-first') {
   257	    node $distributionHelper @distributionArgs --apply
   258	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Plugin-first cleanup refused; repository fallbacks were preserved' }
   259	  }
   260	
   261	  # ---------- verify ----------
   262	
   263	  Push-Location $Directory
   264	  try {
   265	    $statusJson = node .bee\bin\bee.mjs status --json
   266	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Fail 'Verification failed: bee.mjs status did not run.' }
   267	    # Join first: PS 5.1 pipes a multi-line array into ConvertFrom-Json line by line.
   268	    $status = ($statusJson -join "`n") | ConvertFrom-Json
   269	    if (-not $status.onboarding -or $status.onboarding.installed -ne $true) {
   270	      Fail 'Verification failed: bee.mjs status reports not installed.'
   271	    }
   272	    $expectedVersion = (Get-Content (Join-Path $beeSrc '.claude-plugin\plugin.json') -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json).version
   273	    if ($status.onboarding.bee_version -ne $expectedVersion -or
   274	        $status.onboarding.plugin_version -ne $expectedVersion -or
   275	        $status.onboarding.drift -ne $false) {
   276	      Fail "Verification failed: version parity mismatch (expected $expectedVersion; bee $($status.onboarding.bee_version), plugin $($status.onboarding.plugin_version), drift $($status.onboarding.drift))."
   277	    }
   278	    Write-Host "verify   onboarding ok (bee $($status.onboarding.bee_version)), phase: $($status.phase)"
   279	  } finally {
   280	    Pop-Location
   281	  }
   282	
   283	  Write-Host ''
   284	  Write-Host 'bee installed.'
   285	  Write-Host "  next: open an agent session in $Directory"
   286	  Write-Host '  - Claude Code: the session preamble appears via hooks; or say "Route this through bee: <task>"'
   287	  Write-Host '  - Codex: the AGENTS.md BEE block bootstraps; first step is bee.mjs status'
   288	  Write-Host '  - scout any time: node .bee/bin/bee.mjs status --json'
   289	} finally {
   290	  if ($cleanupDir -and (Test-Path $cleanupDir)) {
   291	    Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $cleanupDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   292	  }
   293	  if ($stateTempDir -and (Test-Path $stateTempDir)) {
   294	    Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stateTempDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   295	  }
   296	}

   223	
   224	function collectProjectCleanup(repoRoot, managedSkills) {
   225	  assertPlainDirectory(repoRoot, "repository root");
   226	  if (!(managedSkills instanceof Set) || managedSkills.size === 0) fail("project cleanup requires the managed release skill set");
   227	  const dirs = [];
   228	  const seen = new Set();
   229	  for (const relativeRoot of PROJECT_SKILL_ROOTS) {
   230	    const root = path.join(repoRoot, relativeRoot);
   231	    const stat = lstatOrNull(root);
   232	    if (!stat) continue;
   233	    assertPlainDirectory(root, "project skill root");
   234	    if (!isInside(repoRoot, root)) fail(`project skill root escapes repository: ${root}`);
   235	    for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(root, { withFileTypes: true })) {
   236	      if (!managedSkills.has(entry.name)) continue;
   237	      const target = path.join(root, entry.name);
   238	      if (entry.isSymbolicLink() || !entry.isDirectory()) fail(`managed cleanup target must be a direct plain directory: ${target}`);
   239	      assertPlainDirectory(target, "managed cleanup target");
   240	      const real = fs.realpathSync.native(target);
   241	      if (seen.has(real)) fail(`duplicate cleanup target alias: ${target}`);
   242	      seen.add(real);
   243	      dirs.push(target);
   244	    }
   245	  }
   246	  const configs = PROJECT_HOOK_FILES.map((relative) => cleanHookConfig(path.join(repoRoot, relative))).filter(Boolean);
   247	  return { dirs: dirs.sort(), configs };
   248	}
   249	
   250	function readOwnershipLedger(ledgerPath, requestedRoots) {
   251	  if (!requestedRoots.length) return { dirs: [], update: null };
   252	  if (!ledgerPath) fail("user-root cleanup requires an ownership ledger");
   253	  assertPlainFile(ledgerPath, "ownership ledger");
   254	  let ledger;
   255	  try { ledger = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(ledgerPath, "utf8")); } catch (error) { fail(`ownership ledger is not valid JSON: ${error.message}`); }
   256	  if (ledger?.schemaVersion !== 1 || !Array.isArray(ledger.roots)) fail("ownership ledger has an unsupported shape");
   257	  const requested = requestedRoots.map((root) => path.resolve(root));
   258	  if (new Set(requested).size !== requested.length) fail("user skill roots contain a duplicate or alias");
   259	  const dirs = [];
   260	  const seen = new Set();
   261	  for (const root of requested) {
   262	    assertPlainDirectory(root, "user skill root");
   263	    const matches = ledger.roots.filter((entry) => path.resolve(entry?.path ?? "") === root);
   264	    if (matches.length !== 1 || !Array.isArray(matches[0].skills)) fail(`ownership ledger does not exactly name user root: ${root}`);
   265	    for (const name of matches[0].skills) {
   266	      if (typeof name !== "string" || !/^bee-[a-z0-9-]+$/.test(name)) fail(`ownership ledger contains unsafe skill name: ${name}`);
   267	      const target = path.join(root, name);
   268	      if (!isInside(root, target)) fail(`ledger target escapes root: ${target}`);
   269	      const stat = lstatOrNull(target);
   270	      if (!stat) continue;
   271	      assertPlainDirectory(target, "ledger-owned skill");
   272	      const real = fs.realpathSync.native(target);
   273	      if (seen.has(real)) fail(`duplicate ledger target alias: ${target}`);
   274	      seen.add(real);
   275	      dirs.push(target);
   276	    }
   277	  }
   278	  const next = structuredClone(ledger);
   279	  for (const entry of next.roots) if (requested.includes(path.resolve(entry.path))) entry.skills = [];
   280	  return { dirs, update: { path: ledgerPath, before: fs.readFileSync(ledgerPath), after: Buffer.from(`${JSON.stringify(next, null, 2)}\n`) } };
   281	}
   282	
   283	function snapshotTree(root) {
   284	  const rows = [];
   285	  const walk = (current, relative = "") => {
   286	    const stat = lstatOrNull(current);
   287	    if (!stat || stat.isSymbolicLink()) fail(`snapshot target is missing or symlinked: ${current}`);
   288	    const mode = (stat.mode & 0o777).toString(8).padStart(3, "0");
   289	    if (stat.isFile()) { rows.push([relative, "file", mode, sha256(fs.readFileSync(current))]); return; }
   290	    if (!stat.isDirectory()) fail(`snapshot target has an unsupported entry: ${current}`);
   291	    if (relative) rows.push([relative, "dir", mode]);
   292	    for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(current).sort()) walk(path.join(current, entry), path.join(relative, entry.name ?? entry));
   293	  };
   294	  walk(root);
   295	  return sha256(Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(rows)));
   296	}
   297	
   298	function snapshotTargets(dirs, writes) {
   299	  return {
   300	    dirs: dirs.map((target) => ({ path: target, digest: snapshotTree(target) })),
   301	    writes: writes.map((item) => ({ path: item.path, digest: sha256(item.before) })),
   302	  };
   303	}
   304	
   305	function revalidateSnapshot(snapshot) {
   306	  for (const item of snapshot.dirs) {
   307	    assertPlainDirectory(item.path, "cleanup target");
   308	    const digest = snapshotTree(item.path);
   309	    if (digest !== item.digest) fail(`cleanup target changed after preflight: ${item.path}`);
   310	  }
   311	  for (const item of snapshot.writes) {
   312	    assertPlainFile(item.path, "planned configuration write");
   313	    if (sha256(fs.readFileSync(item.path)) !== item.digest) fail(`configuration changed after preflight: ${item.path}`);
   314	  }
   315	}
   316	
   317	export function buildDistributionPlan({ mode, runtimes, repoRoot, pluginStates, inventory, ledgerPath = null, userSkillRoots = [] }) {
   318	  if (!['plugin-first', 'repo-copy'].includes(mode)) fail(`unknown distribution mode: ${mode}`);
   319	  if (!Array.isArray(runtimes) || runtimes.length === 0) fail("at least one runtime is required");
   320	  const selectedStates = runtimes.map((runtime) => pluginStates.find((state) => state.runtime === runtime) ?? { runtime, installed: false, enabled: false });
   321	  if (mode === "repo-copy") {
   322	    provePluginInactive(selectedStates);
   323	    return { mode, runtimes, status: "ready_for_onboarding", dirs: [], writes: [], snapshot: { dirs: [], writes: [] } };
   324	  }
   325	  const managedSkills = managedSkillNames(inventory);
   326	  const proofs = selectedStates.map((state) => proveInstalledPackage(state, inventory));
   327	  const project = collectProjectCleanup(path.resolve(repoRoot), managedSkills);
   328	  const user = readOwnershipLedger(ledgerPath, userSkillRoots);
   329	  const dirs = [...project.dirs, ...user.dirs];
   330	  const cleanupRealpaths = dirs.map((target) => fs.realpathSync.native(target));
   331	  if (new Set(cleanupRealpaths).size !== cleanupRealpaths.length) fail("cleanup plan contains duplicate or aliased targets");
   332	  const writes = [...project.configs, ...(user.update ? [user.update] : [])];
   333	  const snapshot = snapshotTargets(dirs, writes);
   334	  return { mode, runtimes, status: dirs.length || writes.length ? "changes_needed" : "up_to_date", proofs, dirs, writes, snapshot };
   335	}
   336	
   337	export function applyDistributionPlan(plan) {
   338	  revalidateSnapshot(plan.snapshot);
   339	  if (plan.mode === "repo-copy" || (!plan.dirs.length && !plan.writes.length)) return { status: plan.mode === "repo-copy" ? "ready_for_onboarding" : "up_to_date", removed: 0, updated: 0 };
   340	  const token = randomUUID();
   341	  const moved = [];
   342	  const configMoves = [];
   343	  try {
   344	    for (const target of plan.dirs) {
   345	      const quarantine = `${target}.bee-cleanup-${token}`;
   346	      if (lstatOrNull(quarantine)) fail(`quarantine path already exists: ${quarantine}`);
   347	      fs.renameSync(target, quarantine);
   348	      moved.push({ target, quarantine });
   349	    }
   350	    for (const item of plan.writes) {
   351	      const temp = `${item.path}.bee-write-${token}`;
   352	      const backup = `${item.path}.bee-cleanup-${token}`;
   353	      if (lstatOrNull(temp) || lstatOrNull(backup)) fail(`configuration transaction path already exists: ${item.path}`);
   354	      const originalMode = fs.statSync(item.path).mode & 0o777;
   355	      fs.renameSync(item.path, backup);
   356	      configMoves.push({ item, temp, backup });
   357	      fs.writeFileSync(temp, item.after, { mode: originalMode });
   358	      fs.renameSync(temp, item.path);
   359	    }
   360	  } catch (error) {
   361	    for (const config of configMoves.reverse()) {
   362	      fs.rmSync(config.temp, { force: true });
   363	      fs.rmSync(config.item.path, { force: true });
   364	      if (lstatOrNull(config.backup)) fs.renameSync(config.backup, config.item.path);
   365	    }
   366	    for (const item of moved.reverse()) if (lstatOrNull(item.quarantine) && !lstatOrNull(item.target)) fs.renameSync(item.quarantine, item.target);
   367	    throw error;
   368	  }
   369	  for (const item of moved) fs.rmSync(item.quarantine, { recursive: true, force: true });
   370	  for (const config of configMoves) fs.rmSync(config.backup, { force: true });
   371	  return { status: "applied", removed: moved.length, updated: configMoves.length };
   372	}
   373	
   374	function parseArgs(argv) {
   375	  const options = { apply: false, userSkillRoots: [] };
   376	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
   377	    const arg = argv[i];
   378	    if (arg === "--apply") options.apply = true;
   379	    else if (arg === "--user-skill-root") options.userSkillRoots.push(argv[++i]);
   380	    else if (arg.startsWith("--")) options[arg.slice(2).replaceAll("-", "_")] = argv[++i];
   381	    else fail(`unexpected argument: ${arg}`);
   382	  }
   383	  return options;
   384	}
   385	
   386	function parseRuntime(value) {
   387	  if (value === "both") return ["claude", "codex"];
   388	  if (["claude", "codex"].includes(value)) return [value];
   389	  fail("--runtime must be claude, codex, or both");
   390	}
   391	
   392	function runCli() {
   393	  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
   394	  const runtimes = parseRuntime(args.runtime);
   395	  if (!args.mode || !args.repo_root || !args.release_manifest || !args.plugin_state_file) fail("--mode, --runtime, --repo-root, --release-manifest, and --plugin-state-file are required");
   396	  // Strip a leading UTF-8 BOM (U+FEFF) before parsing: install.ps1 writes this
   397	  // state file via PowerShell `Set-Content -Encoding UTF8`, which on PS 5.1
   398	  // prepends a BOM, and a bare JSON.parse then throws "Unexpected token" on it —
   399	  // surfaced as "Distribution preflight refused" and a broken Windows install (#9).
   400	  const payload = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(args.plugin_state_file, "utf8").replace(/^\uFEFF/, ""));
   401	  const pluginStates = runtimes.map((runtime) => discoverBeePlugin(payload?.[runtime] ?? payload, runtime));
   402	  const inventory = loadPackageInventory(args.release_manifest);
   403	  const plan = buildDistributionPlan({ mode: args.mode, runtimes, repoRoot: args.repo_root, pluginStates, inventory, ledgerPath: args.ledger, userSkillRoots: args.userSkillRoots });
   404	  const result = args.apply ? applyDistributionPlan(plan) : { status: plan.status, removed: plan.dirs.length, updated: plan.writes.length };
   405	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({ ok: true, mode: args.mode, runtimes, dryRun: !args.apply, ...result })}\n`);
   406	}
   407	
   408	const isMain = process.argv[1] && path.resolve(process.argv[1]) === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
   409	if (isMain) {
   410	  try { runCli(); } catch (error) {
   411	    process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({ ok: false, status: "blocked", error: error.message })}\n`);
   412	    process.exitCode = 1;
   413	  }
   414	}

exec
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs | sed -n '90,230p'; nl -ba skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs | sed -n '1820,1885p'; rg -n \"real.*skills|source tree|hashTree\\(.*skills|byte-identical|deep mirror|render\" scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs scripts/*.mjs | head -n 300" in /home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog
 succeeded in 255ms:
    90	  fs.writeFileSync(
    91	    hangingModule,
    92	    'process.stdout.write("hanging stdout\\n"); process.stderr.write("hanging stderr\\n"); setInterval(() => {}, 1_000);\n',
    93	  );
    94	  const timedOut = await runModuleWorker(hangingModule, { timeout: 100 });
    95	  check(timedOut.status === null, "shared Worker timeout returns null status");
    96	  check(timedOut.signal === "SIGTERM", "shared Worker timeout returns SIGTERM signal");
    97	  check(timedOut.error?.code === "ETIMEDOUT", "shared Worker timeout returns ETIMEDOUT error");
    98	  check(
    99	    timedOut.stdout.includes("hanging stdout") && timedOut.stderr.includes("hanging stderr"),
   100	    "shared Worker timeout preserves captured stdout and stderr",
   101	  );
   102	
   103	  // --- 1. plan mode on empty repo -> changes_needed -----------------------
   104	  const plan1 = await runOnboard(["--repo-root", tmp, "--json"], tmpHome);
   105	  check(plan1.status === 0, "plan mode exits 0", plan1.stderr);
   106	  check(plan1.payload?.status === "changes_needed", "empty repo reports changes_needed",
   107	    `got: ${plan1.payload?.status}`);
   108	  check(Array.isArray(plan1.payload?.plan) && plan1.payload.plan.length > 0,
   109	    "plan mode lists planned actions");
   110	  check(!fs.existsSync(path.join(tmp, "AGENTS.md")), "plan mode writes nothing");
   111	  const plan1Actions = (plan1.payload?.plan || []).map((i) => i.action);
   112	  check(plan1Actions.includes("create_agents_block") &&
   113	    plan1Actions.includes("propose_agents_header"),
   114	    "empty repo plans create_agents_block + propose_agents_header (D4)",
   115	    JSON.stringify(plan1Actions));
   116	  check(plan1Actions.indexOf("propose_agents_header") >
   117	    plan1Actions.indexOf("create_agents_block"),
   118	    "propose_agents_header ordered after create_agents_block");
   119	
   120	  // --- 2. apply ------------------------------------------------------------
   121	  const apply1 = await runOnboard(["--repo-root", tmp, "--apply", "--json"], tmpHome);
   122	  check(apply1.status === 0, "apply exits 0", apply1.stderr);
   123	  check(apply1.payload?.status === "applied", "apply reports applied");
   124	  check(apply1.payload?.recheck === "up_to_date", "apply recheck is up_to_date",
   125	    JSON.stringify(apply1.payload?.recheck_plan || []));
   126	
   127	  // --- 2b. per-project skill roots (installer-hardening D2/D6) --------------
   128	  // A fresh host onboard --apply materializes the full bee-* skill set under
   129	  // BOTH <repo>/.claude/skills and <repo>/.agents/skills by default, never
   130	  // touches ~/.claude/skills without --global-skills, and never gitignores
   131	  // the committed trees (D4).
   132	  const SOURCE_SKILLS_ROOT = path.join(REPO_ROOT, "skills");
   133	  const sourceBeeSkills = fs.readdirSync(SOURCE_SKILLS_ROOT, { withFileTypes: true })
   134	    .filter((e) => e.isDirectory() && e.name.startsWith("bee-"))
   135	    .map((e) => e.name)
   136	    .sort();
   137	  check(sourceBeeSkills.length === 15,
   138	    "source tree carries the expected 15 bee-* skills", JSON.stringify(sourceBeeSkills));
   139	  for (const relRoot of [".claude/skills", ".agents/skills"]) {
   140	    const rootAbs = path.join(tmp, ...relRoot.split("/"));
   141	    const installed = fs.existsSync(rootAbs)
   142	      ? fs.readdirSync(rootAbs, { withFileTypes: true })
   143	          .filter((e) => e.isDirectory() && e.name.startsWith("bee-"))
   144	          .map((e) => e.name)
   145	          .sort()
   146	      : [];
   147	    check(JSON.stringify(installed) === JSON.stringify(sourceBeeSkills),
   148	      `fresh host apply materializes all 15 bee-* dirs under ${relRoot}`,
   149	      JSON.stringify(installed));
   150	  }
   151	  const applyTargetKinds = (apply1.payload?.skills?.targets || []).map((t) => t.kind);
   152	  check(JSON.stringify(applyTargetKinds) === JSON.stringify(["repo-claude", "repo-agents"]),
   153	    "default apply reports exactly the two in-repo targets (no global without --global-skills)",
   154	    JSON.stringify(applyTargetKinds));
   155	  check(!fs.existsSync(path.join(tmpHome, ".claude", "skills")),
   156	    "without --global-skills, ~/.claude/skills is never written");
   157	  const gitignoreAfterApply = fs.readFileSync(path.join(tmp, ".gitignore"), "utf8");
   158	  check(!gitignoreAfterApply.includes(".claude/skills") &&
   159	    !gitignoreAfterApply.includes(".agents/skills"),
   160	    "per-project skill trees are never gitignored (D4: committed to the host repo)",
   161	    gitignoreAfterApply);
   162	
   163	  // --- 2c. bee agent files (config-rendered pinned agent types, Slice 3B) ---
   164	  // Fresh repo, default config.json (extraction: haiku, generation: sonnet,
   165	  // review unset -> AGENT_TIER_DEFAULTS_CLAUDE fallback opus). must_haves:
   166	  // "three agent files render into .claude/agents/ with model values taken
   167	  // from live config, never hardcoded in the template output path".
   168	  const agentFilePath = (name) => path.join(tmp, ".claude", "agents", `${name}.md`);
   169	  for (const [name, tier, model] of [
   170	    ["bee-gather", "generation", "sonnet"],
   171	    ["bee-extract", "extraction", "haiku"],
   172	    ["bee-review", "review", "opus"],
   173	  ]) {
   174	    check(fs.existsSync(agentFilePath(name)), `${name}.md is rendered on fresh apply`);
   175	    const text = fs.existsSync(agentFilePath(name)) ? fs.readFileSync(agentFilePath(name), "utf8") : "";
   176	    check(text.includes(`name: ${name}`), `${name}.md frontmatter carries its own name`, text);
   177	    check(text.includes(`model: ${model}`),
   178	      `${name}.md frontmatter model comes from the configured ${tier} tier (${model}), not a hardcoded pin`,
   179	      text);
   180	    check(!text.includes("{{TIER_MODEL}}"), `${name}.md has no unrendered {{TIER_MODEL}} placeholder`, text);
   181	    check(!/\bcost[- ]?reduc/i.test(text), `${name}.md makes no cost-reduction claim`, text);
   182	  }
   183	
   184	  // AO10: agents root is never joined to REPO_SKILL_TARGETS (regression guard
   185	  // against the exact change plan.md flagged as forbidden).
   186	  const onboardSourceForRegressionGuard = fs.readFileSync(path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, "onboard_bee.mjs"), "utf8");
   187	  const repoSkillTargetsMatch = onboardSourceForRegressionGuard.match(/REPO_SKILL_TARGETS = \[([\s\S]*?)\];/);
   188	  // "repo-agents" (the existing .agents/skills target's `kind`) legitimately
   189	  // contains the substring "agents" - the forbidden shape is a `segments`
   190	  // array naming a plain "agents" directory (an agents ROOT, e.g. `[".claude",
   191	  // "agents"]`), not the `kind` label. Match on `segments` arrays only.
   192	  const repoSkillTargetsSegments = [...(repoSkillTargetsMatch?.[1] || "").matchAll(/segments:\s*\[([^\]]*)\]/g)]
   193	    .map((m) => m[1]);
   194	  check(Boolean(repoSkillTargetsMatch) && repoSkillTargetsSegments.length === 2 &&
   195	    repoSkillTargetsSegments.every((s) => !/"agents"/.test(s)),
   196	    "REPO_SKILL_TARGETS is untouched - no agents-root segments entry (AO10)",
   197	    JSON.stringify(repoSkillTargetsSegments));
   198	
   199	  // AO11: Codex gets no agent files at all - the asymmetry is recorded, not
   200	  // silently absent.
   201	  check(!fs.existsSync(path.join(tmp, ".agents", "agents")),
   202	    ".agents/ receives no agent files (AO11 - no .agents/agents root at all)");
   203	  const onboardingAfterApply1 = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path.join(tmp, ".bee", "onboarding.json"), "utf8"));
   204	  check(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.bee_version === apply1.payload?.bee_version,
   205	    "onboarding.json agents_sync carries the sync's own bee_version marker",
   206	    JSON.stringify(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync));
   207	  check(JSON.stringify((onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.files || []).slice().sort()) ===
   208	    JSON.stringify([".claude/agents/bee-extract.md", ".claude/agents/bee-gather.md", ".claude/agents/bee-review.md"]),
   209	    "onboarding.json agents_sync.files names all three rendered agent files",
   210	    JSON.stringify(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.files));
   211	  const sortedEntries = (o) => JSON.stringify(Object.entries(o || {}).sort());
   212	  check(sortedEntries(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.rendered_from) ===
   213	    sortedEntries({ generation: "sonnet", extraction: "haiku", review: "opus" }),
   214	    "onboarding.json agents_sync.rendered_from records {tier: model} from live config",
   215	    JSON.stringify(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.rendered_from));
   216	  check(Array.isArray(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.codex?.agents) &&
   217	    onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync.codex.agents.length === 0 &&
   218	    /AO11/.test(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync.codex.note || ""),
   219	    "onboarding.json agents_sync records the Codex asymmetry (AO11) inline, no separate file",
   220	    JSON.stringify(onboardingAfterApply1.agents_sync?.codex));
   221	
   222	  // Idempotency: must_haves "re-running self-onboard is idempotent (same
   223	  // bytes, marker refreshed)". Snapshot bytes, re-apply, compare.
   224	  const agentBytesBefore = {
   225	    "bee-gather": fs.readFileSync(agentFilePath("bee-gather")),
   226	    "bee-extract": fs.readFileSync(agentFilePath("bee-extract")),
   227	    "bee-review": fs.readFileSync(agentFilePath("bee-review")),
   228	  };
   229	  const reapplyForIdempotency = await runOnboard(["--repo-root", tmp, "--apply", "--json"], tmpHome);
   230	  check(reapplyForIdempotency.payload?.status === "applied", "idempotency re-apply succeeds");
  1820	        apply.payload.reason.includes(scenario.reasonNeedle),
  1821	      `source tuple: ${scenario.label} names the strict release-identity failure`,
  1822	      String(apply.payload?.reason),
  1823	    );
  1824	    check(
  1825	      hashTree(repo) === repoBefore && hashTree(home) === homeBefore,
  1826	      `source tuple: ${scenario.label} leaves repo and runtime targets byte-identical`,
  1827	    );
  1828	    check(
  1829	      apply.payload?.forced_downgrade === undefined,
  1830	      `source tuple: ${scenario.label} is never forceable`,
  1831	    );
  1832	  } finally {
  1833	    fs.rmSync(base, { recursive: true, force: true });
  1834	    fs.rmSync(home, { recursive: true, force: true });
  1835	  }
  1836	}
  1837	
  1838	// --- 10a2. drift in ONE in-repo root -> sync_skill for that root only --------
  1839	{
  1840	  const base = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "bee-skillsync-onedrift-"));
  1841	  const home = makeFakeHome();
  1842	  try {
  1843	    const { launcher } = makeFakeSkillsRoot(path.join(base, "skills"), {
  1844	      skills: { "bee-alpha": { "SKILL.md": "# alpha v1\n" } },
  1845	    });
  1846	    const repo = path.join(base, "repo");
  1847	    fs.mkdirSync(repo, { recursive: true });
  1848	    // A host repo that merely contains its own skills/ dir never trips the
  1849	    // self-onboard skip: onboarding runs from an external source.
  1850	    fs.mkdirSync(path.join(repo, "skills", "bee-decoy"), { recursive: true });
  1851	    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(repo, "skills", "bee-decoy", "SKILL.md"), "# decoy\n", "utf8");
  1852	    await runOnboardAt(launcher, ["--repo-root", repo, "--apply", "--json"], home);
  1853	    const clean = await runOnboardAt(launcher, ["--repo-root", repo, "--json"], home);
  1854	    check(clean.payload?.status === "up_to_date",
  1855	      "one-root drift: immediate re-run reports up_to_date (repo skills/ dir never trips self-skip)",
  1856	      JSON.stringify(clean.payload?.plan || []));
  1857	    fs.writeFileSync(
  1858	      path.join(repo, ".agents", "skills", "bee-alpha", "SKILL.md"), "# drifted\n", "utf8");
  1859	    const plan = await runOnboardAt(launcher, ["--repo-root", repo, "--json"], home);
  1860	    const driftItems = (plan.payload?.plan || []).filter((i) => i.action === "sync_skill");
  1861	    check(plan.payload?.status === "changes_needed" &&
  1862	      driftItems.length === 1 && driftItems[0].skill === "bee-alpha" &&
  1863	      driftItems[0].target === "repo-agents",
  1864	      "drift planted in ONE root yields sync_skill for exactly that root",
  1865	      JSON.stringify(driftItems));
  1866	    const heal = await runOnboardAt(launcher, ["--repo-root", repo, "--apply", "--json"], home);
  1867	    check(heal.payload?.status === "applied" && heal.payload?.recheck === "up_to_date",
  1868	      "one-root drift: apply heals it, recheck up_to_date");
  1869	    check(readRepoTarget(repo, ".agents/skills", "bee-alpha/SKILL.md") === "# alpha v1\n",
  1870	      "one-root drift: drifted file mirrored back to source bytes");
  1871	  } finally {
  1872	    try {
  1873	      fs.rmSync(base, { recursive: true, force: true });
  1874	      fs.rmSync(home, { recursive: true, force: true });
  1875	    } catch {
  1876	      // best-effort cleanup
  1877	    }
  1878	  }
  1879	}

  1885	// Regression guard: a repo that ships hooks/catalog.mjs must NEVER have its

  8266	// worktree-isolation-3: attested dispatch contract (D1/D3)
  8267	await check('worktree dispatch contract: eligibility, protected attestation, consistency-only identity, and typed integration halts are explicit', async () => {
  8268	  const templatesRoot = fileURLToPath(new URL('..', import.meta.url));
  8269	  const repoRoot = findRepoRoot(templatesRoot);

  8274	    path.join(repoRoot, 'skills', 'bee-executing', 'references', 'worker-details.md'),
  8275	    'utf8',
  8276	  );
  8277	
  8278	  assert(/worktree-isolation-1\s*→\s*worktree-isolation-2\s*→\s*worktree-isolation-3/.test(swarming), 'the enabling sequence must be serialized in the shared checkout');
  8279	  assert(/Claude Code[\s\S]{0,80}wave[\s\S]{0,80}(?:at\s+least\s+two|>=\s*2|≥\s*2)/i.test(swarming), 'normal native isolation must require a Claude Code wave with at least two workers');
  8280	  assert(/worktree-isolation-4[\s\S]{0,100}(?:sole|only)[\s\S]{0,80}(?:one-worker|single-worker)[\s\S]{0,80}(?:exception|acceptance)/i.test(swarming), 'wt-4 must be the sole one-worker validation exception');
  8281	
  8282	  for (const field of ['commonDir', 'worktreePath', 'worktreeId', 'headRef', 'baseCommit', 'declaredPaths', 'reservedPaths']) {
  8283	    assert(swarming.includes(`\`${field}\``), `protected pre-dispatch attestation must name ${field}`);
  8284	  }
  8285	  assert(/before[\s\S]{0,100}(?:worker output|worker result)/i.test(swarming), 'attestation must be captured before worker output exists');
  8286	  assert(/(?:cannot|unable to)[\s\S]{0,80}(?:capture|retain)[\s\S]{0,80}attestation[\s\S]{0,100}(?:ineligible|refus)/i.test(swarming), 'worktree mode must refuse runtimes that cannot capture and retain the attestation');
  8287	  assert(/same-UID[\s\S]{0,80}(?:cooperative|fallible)[\s\S]{0,80}not[\s\S]{0,80}security principal/i.test(swarming), 'the same-UID worker threat model must be explicit');
  8288	  assert(/Git\s+metadata[\s\S]{0,100}consistency\s+evidence[\s\S]{0,100}(?:not|never)[\s\S]{0,100}(?:authorization|security)/i.test(swarming), 'Git metadata must be consistency evidence rather than authorization');
  8289	  assert(/merge-base --is-ancestor/.test(swarming), 'integration must prove the candidate commit descends from the attested base');
  8290	  assert(/diff[\s\S]{0,160}(?:subset|contained)[\s\S]{0,100}reservedPaths/i.test(swarming), 'integration must constrain the base-to-candidate diff to attested reservations');

  8292	  for (const code of ['WORKTREE_ATTESTATION_UNAVAILABLE', 'WORKTREE_IDENTITY_MISMATCH', 'WORKTREE_BASE_ANCESTRY_MISMATCH', 'WORKTREE_RESERVED_DIFF_MISMATCH']) {
  8293	    assert(swarming.includes(`\`${code}\``), `typed worktree halt missing: ${code}`);

  8296	  assert(/backlink[\s\S]{0,100}WORKTREE_IDENTITY_MISMATCH/i.test(swarming), 'backlink mismatch must halt as a typed identity mismatch');

  8301	// worktree-isolation-4: transactional integration/disposition acceptance (D3)
  8302	function git(cwd, args, { allowFailure = false } = {}) {
  8303	  const result = spawnSync('git', args, { cwd, encoding: 'utf8' });
  8304	  if (!allowFailure && result.status !== 0) {
  8305	    throw new Error(`git ${args.join(' ')} failed (${result.status}): ${result.stderr || result.stdout}`);
  8470	
  8471	    const green = makeWorktreeTransactionFixture(); fixtures.push(green.fixtureRoot);
  8472	    const greenResult = integrateFixture(green);
  8473	    assert(greenResult.code === 'WORKTREE_INTEGRATED', 'green transaction must integrate');
  8474	    assert(greenResult.provenance.pwd === fs.realpathSync(green.main), 'provenance must identify the main checkout');
  8475	    assert(greenResult.provenance.preMain !== greenResult.provenance.postMain, 'provenance must capture pre/post main HEAD');
  8476	    assert(greenResult.provenance.mergedCommitIsAncestor, 'provenance must prove merged candidate ancestry');
  8477	    assert(greenResult.cleanup && !fs.existsSync(green.worktree), 'green reachable clean worker may be removed without force');
  8478	    assert(git(green.main, ['show-ref', '--verify', `refs/heads/${green.branch}`], { allowFailure: true }).status !== 0, 'green worker branch must be deleted with branch -d');
  8479	  } finally {
  8480	    for (const fixtureRoot of fixtures) fs.rmSync(fixtureRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
  8481	  }
  8482	});
  8483	
  8484	await check('worktree transactional contract: provenance, conservative cleanup, and authorized recovery are explicit', async () => {
  8485	  const templatesRoot = fileURLToPath(new URL('..', import.meta.url));
  8486	  const repoRoot = findRepoRoot(templatesRoot);
  8487	  if (!repoRoot) return;

  8489	
  8490	  assert(/git merge --no-ff --no-commit/.test(reference), 'transaction must use --no-ff --no-commit');
  8491	  assert(/git merge --abort[\s\S]{0,180}(?:conflict|targeted)[\s\S]{0,100}(?:pre-main|history)/i.test(reference), 'conflict and targeted red must abort without changing pre-main history');
  8492	  for (const field of ['pwd', 'pre-main HEAD', 'post-main HEAD', 'merged-commit ancestry', 'exact full repository verify command', 'full verify output']) {
  8493	    assert(reference.includes(field), `committed-main provenance must include ${field}`);
  8494	  }
  8495	  assert(/post-commit[\s\S]{0,100}red[\s\S]{0,180}git revert[\s\S]{0,120}before any later work/i.test(reference), 'postcommit red must immediately create a non-destructive revert');
  8496	  assert(/git status --porcelain[\s\S]{0,220}green[\s\S]{0,180}reachable[\s\S]{0,180}git worktree remove[\s\S]{0,100}git branch -d/i.test(reference), 'automatic cleanup must require clean, green, reachable state and non-force commands');
  8497	  assert(!/git worktree remove\s+--force|git branch\s+-D/.test(reference), 'automatic cleanup contract must not use force removal/deletion');
  8498	  for (const outcome of ['`[BLOCKED]`', '`[HANDOFF]`', 'abandonment', 'identity mismatch', 'merge conflict', 'red verification']) {
  8499	    assert(reference.includes(outcome), `${outcome} must suppress cleanup and preserve recovery identity`);
  8500	  }
  8501	  assert(/explicit operator authorization[\s\S]{0,220}status[\s\S]{0,120}dirty\/untracked diff[\s\S]{0,120}HEAD[\s\S]{0,120}reachability[\s\S]{0,180}(?:recovery ref|patch)/i.test(reference), 'destructive drop must require operator authorization and complete recovery capture');
  8502	});

Nesting is refused (a bee:only inside a bee:only is a hard validation error at sync time, loud, zero writes for that skill); an unclosed marker likewise. Decide and implement how per-target version markers/hashes account for rendered content (each target hashes its OWN rendered bytes — never the source bytes — so drift detection stays honest per root), and how bee's own repo gets rendered copies given self_skip (read the current mechanism that keeps .claude/skills + .agents/skills byte-identical to skills/ in THIS repo and extend it to render — likely the same sync path host repos use; find it, do not guess). Write scripts/test_skill_render.mjs: unit tests for the filter (claude-only block, codex-only block, both, none, marker stripping, nesting refusal, unclosed refusal, idempotent re-render) plus an integration assertion that a fixture skill with blocks renders differently per target and identically on re-run. Update test_onboard_bee.mjs / test_plugin_distribution.mjs expectations ONLY where the render model legitimately changes them — document each changed expectation in the cell report. No skill content is tagged in this cell (that is cnr2-10/11); with zero markers present, rendered output MUST be byte-identical to source — assert that explicitly (the no-marker no-op invariant is the regression net).",

skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md-58-
skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md-59-## Plan Checker (adversarial)
skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md-60-
skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md:61:Dispatch a subagent on the **`review` slot** (decision 0021 — `resolveTier(root, 'review', runtime)`, default opus on Claude, generation fallback; state the model explicitly; if the runtime cannot select per-agent models, cap its reads and output instead). On Claude Code, spawn `subagent_type: "bee-review"` when `.claude/agents/bee-review.md` exists (W3, AO5/AO10) — bee's own rendered agent for the review tier, never `general-purpose` (`bee-model-guard` denies that pairing); Codex has no per-agent subagent type (AO11), so the tier stays enforced as a read budget + output cap only. The plan-checker is a **read-only gather**, never a cell — when the review slot is cli-shaped, resolve it with the purpose-scoped 4-arg form, `resolveTier(root, 'review', runtime, {for:'gather'})`, per the Delegation contract's cli gather branch (`bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md`); a bare 3-arg resolve of a cli-shaped review slot now refuses (AO12/B1, plan 2A-ii). A model-shaped review slot is unaffected by purpose — dispatch it exactly as before — **in the background where the runtime supports it** (decision 0017): continue the spike/matrix/cell-review work while it runs; its findings block nothing until the Gate 3 presentation, which never happens with the checker still outstanding. It assumes the plan is flawed and verifies 5 dimensions: requirement/decision coverage, cell completeness, dependency correctness, key links, scope sanity. Every finding carries **BLOCKER** or **WARNING**. Maximum 3 structural-verification iterations; a BLOCKER still open after iteration 3 escalates to the user. Never attempt iteration 4.
skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md-62-
skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md-63-**High-risk lane:** scale to a persona panel — coherence + feasibility lenses always, plus conditional lenses (security, product, scope-guardian) chosen by the diff of concerns. Dedupe findings, then synthesize into auto-fix vs present-for-decision buckets.

skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md-88-**Evidence bundle (mandatory, every consult):** exact failing command, the failing output, your diagnosis, the relevant cited file excerpts, and the `CONTEXT.md` path. Pass it **inline in the consult prompt or via stdin — never a `/tmp` path** (critical pattern 20260708). Never include secrets or env values.
skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md-89-
skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md-90-**Transport** — the `Advisor` line names the advisor and how to consult it:
skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md:91:- **Model-shaped advisor:** consult via your own Agent tool, with the model param set to the named advisor model, and the dispatch `description` starting **exactly** `advisor-consult <cell-id>: <advisor-model>` — this is the A2 attribution record; bee-swarming's goal-check reads it from `.bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl`. Fallback if Agent dispatch is unavailable or rejected: a headless one-shot `claude -p --model <advisor-model>` call, same evidence bundle via stdin.
skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md-92-- **cli-shaped advisor:** run the given command with the evidence bundle on stdin, reusing the External Executors output-capture discipline.
skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md-93-- A **transport error** (non-zero exit, rejected dispatch, a hang past the External Executors timeout discipline) is **not advice** — it burns at most **one** budget slot total for the whole claim, and is never retried in a storm. Continue to the next step of the loop, or `[BLOCKED]` once the budget is spent.

skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-117-
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-118-| | Claude Code | Codex |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-119-|---|---|---|
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:120:| Spawn | `Agent` tool, one call per worker; put the worker prompt in `prompt`; set `run_in_background: true` so the whole wave runs in parallel (send all spawns of a wave in one message) | `spawn_agent({task_name: "<stable-name>", message: "<WORKER_PROMPT>", fork_turns: "none"})` (ORCH-01) |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:121:| Model tier | `model` parameter per Agent call = `config.models.claude[tier]` (default `haiku`/`sonnet`/`fable`; ceiling = the orchestrator's model, kept scarce) | `config.models.codex[tier]` if set; today Codex cannot select a per-agent model → tier is enforced as a read budget + output cap in the prompt |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:122:| Result collection | You are notified when each background agent completes; its final message is the worker report — parse the leading status token | Status tokens arrive in the parent thread; use `wait_agent(..., timeout_ms=60000)` only when a specific result is needed |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:123:| Follow-up / rescue | `SendMessage` to the same agent id continues it with context intact; a new `Agent` call starts fresh | `followup_task({target: "<agent id or task name>", message: "..."})` to continue the same agent; a fresh `spawn_agent` only for a genuinely new task — no routine `send_input(...)` mid-flight |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-124-| Harness assist | `bee-chain-nudge` hook fires on SubagentStop: collect the status, update the cell, check reservations | None — the tend loop in this skill is the nudge |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-125-| Isolation guarantee | Fresh context per Agent call; include only the contract fields | `fork_turns: "none"`; never fork the parent history for routine cells (ORCH-02) |
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:126:| Subagent type (W3, AO5/AO10/AO11) | `subagent_type: "bee-gather"`/`"bee-extract"`/`"bee-review"` for generation/extraction/review, when the rendered agent exists (`.claude/agents/bee-*.md`); `ceiling`, and any tier whose slot is cli-shaped or otherwise unrendered, use the runtime default (`general-purpose`) | No per-agent subagent type — the tier is enforced as a read budget + output cap in the worker prompt regardless of what is spawned (documented asymmetry, not parity) |

skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:128:On both runtimes the integrity rails are identical because they live in the helpers: `bee.mjs cells cap` refuses without a verify pass, and `bee.mjs reservations reserve` reports conflicts the worker must turn into `[BLOCKED]`. On Claude Code, `bee-model-guard` additionally denies pairing a `[bee-tier: generation|extraction|review]` marker with `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` (`generic-type-denied`) — the pinned type above is not optional guidance, it is enforced at dispatch.

skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-134-urgency, and no-chatter instructions create no exception. Before a later bounded

skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-158-- **ceiling** = the strongest model in play = **the session model itself** (no config entry). A ceiling cell inherits the session model — omit the `model` param **and** carry the `[bee-tier: ceiling]` marker, anchored to the first non-whitespace token of the prompt or the start of the description (decision 0023 — a marker anywhere else never counts). Keep it scarce: planning, integration, architecture, final review only. Touch it on every dispatch and the saving evaporates.
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-159-- **generation** = the mid worker that runs the loops (implementation, test writing). Where the bulk of dispatches go.

skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-250-
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md:251:The `Advisor` line is omitted entirely — a session whose config has no advisor slot dispatches byte-identical prompts to today — whenever no advisor resolves, or the advisor's model name literally matches the worker's own resolved model (the one honest no-op). Ceiling-tier workers are no longer a skip condition — config is the authority and the orchestrator does not second-guess it with a strength ladder (AO5). The same-model no-op is the orchestrator's, run at dispatch, never left to the worker (AO4 + AO5). When present, `<TRANSPORT>` states the proven transport verbatim, matching what bee-executing's Advisor Consult section tells the worker to run: for a **model-shaped** advisor, `your own Agent tool, model param <advisor-model>, description starting exactly "advisor-consult <CELL_ID>: <advisor-model>"` (fallback: headless `claude -p --model <advisor-model>`); for a **cli-shaped** advisor, `<the configured command>, evidence bundle on stdin` (External Executors output-capture discipline, above).
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-252-
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-253-Never include session history, other cells, or the orchestrator's reasoning. If a worker needs more than this contract, the cell failed cold-pickup review — route the gap back, do not widen the prompt with transcript.
skills/bee-swarming/references/swarming-reference.md-254-
--
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-224-- **Execution worker (AO14, second named class)** — the Delegation contract's other dispatch shape, distinguished from the I/O-offload worker by **authority and state effects**, not by task size. Unlike an I/O worker, an execution worker **does** register in the swarm registry (`bee.mjs state worker add`) and **does** take reservations under its own nickname; it implements exactly one assigned cell (claim → read `read_first` → implement within `files` → verify → cap → release) and returns exactly one status token (`[DONE]`/`[BLOCKED]`/`[HANDOFF]`/`[NOOP]`) — it is authority-bearing, never a digest-only gather. Every `bee-swarming` worker dispatch belongs to this class: full waves in `standard`/`high-risk`, and, since AO14, the single dispatched worker that now carries out `tiny`/`small` cell implementation too (`bee-swarming/SKILL.md`'s Single execution worker section) — never zero of them, even for the lightest lane. An independent reviewer or checker (plan-checker, cell reviewer, panel member) is **neither** class: it is a review-class dispatch — read-only, no registry entry, no reservations, no cell of its own — and is never called an "execution worker."
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-225-- **cli gather branch (plan 2A-ii, decision 34398e69)** — when `resolveTier(root, 'generation', runtime, {for:'gather'}).type === 'cli'`, a gather dispatch runs the configured command **verbatim** via the shell — nothing appended, ever (W7); the prompt goes in on **stdin**; every path handed to the worker is **absolute** (W9); the run is **read-only** by contract. **Stdout IS the digest**, framed by a delimiter contract: the worker prompt instructs the CLI to emit its digest between `<<<BEE_DIGEST` and `BEE_DIGEST>>>` lines, and the orchestrator extracts only what sits between them — missing delimiters or an empty digest is a **failed run**, surfaced loudly, never accepted as a silent green (fail-open-masking pattern, critical-patterns 20260716-class). No `result.json`, no cell, no reservation, no `bee.mjs state worker add` registration for a gather, same as any other I/O worker. **Known measurement gap, named not solved here:** a Bash-launched gather emits zero `dispatch.jsonl` rows (W-d) — closing that gap is Slice 3's job, not this branch's.
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-226-

skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md:230:gathers, a completed `wait_agent` call with no completion is an **empty wait**:
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-231-it is a timeout signal only, never failure. Never follow an empty wait directly
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md:232:with another `wait_agent`; authority, urgency, and no-chatter instructions create
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-233-no exception. Before any later bounded wait, continue material task-local work
skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md-234-when any remains; otherwise take exactly one `list_agents` snapshot. Then send
