skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-89-
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-90-## Operating Contract
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-91-
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:92:1. **Wave analysis.** Run `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs cells schedule --json`: the computed waves are the **default** dispatch order — override only with a stated reason recorded in the swarm report. Refuse to dispatch when diagnostics report cycles. Two ready cells sharing a file means fix the reservations or split the cell scope — never "spawn both and be careful"; the schedule already auto-serializes file overlap into a later wave rather than refusing it. The schedule computation and verify-output capture delegate as extraction-tier I/O workers per the Delegation contract (D2/D3, `bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md`); judgment (assignment, tier choice, goal-check verdicts, override decisions) stays on the orchestrator.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-93-2. **Assign.** The orchestrator picks exactly **one cell per worker**. Workers never self-select, browse the ready list, or take a second cell.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:94:3. **Spawn with the isolation contract.** Each worker prompt contains: the cell id, the path to `docs/history/<feature>/CONTEXT.md` and `docs/history/<feature>/plan.md`, the global constraints, its reservation identity (agent nickname), and the status-token protocol (`[DONE] [BLOCKED] [HANDOFF] [NOOP]`) — **nothing else, never session history**. Use the template in `references/swarming-reference.md`. **Spawn the tier-matched pinned type when its rendered agent exists** (W3, AO5/AO10/AO11): `subagent_type: "bee-gather"` for `generation`, `"bee-extract"` for `extraction`, `"bee-review"` for `review` — these are bee's own rendered agent definitions (`.claude/agents/bee-*.md`, config-sourced at onboarding), never another plugin's type. `ceiling` has no rendered agent (it IS the session model) — spawn it as the runtime's default/general subagent type; the same default applies when the tier's slot is cli-shaped or otherwise has no rendered file. NEVER pair a `[bee-tier: generation|extraction|review]` marker with `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` — `bee-model-guard` denies it (`generic-type-denied`, decision 0023/AO5) precisely so this rule cannot be skipped by habit. Codex has no per-agent `subagent_type` equivalent (AO11 asymmetry) — its tier stays enforced as a read budget + output cap only, exactly as before. NEVER spawn any OTHER plugin's agent type, even when the name matches the role: a same-named agent carries a different contract and makes the run depend on what happens to be installed.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-95-4. **Judge each cell's model tier at dispatch** — you (the orchestrator) assess the task in front of you and pick the fitting tier; it is NOT fixed by planning (a planning `tier` is at most a hint you may override; decision 0016). Rubric from the cell's lane + action + must_haves + files:
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-96-   - **extraction** — pure retrieval or mechanical edits: rename, reformat, move a file, a one-line change, no design judgment.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-97-   - **generation** — normal implementation, wiring, writing tests: the default for most cells.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-98-   - **ceiling** — integration across modules, architecture/design calls, security-sensitive or `high-risk`-lane work, ambiguous specs, cross-cutting change: where a wrong call is expensive.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-99-
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:100:   Record the choice so scarcity stays measurable: `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs cells tier --id <id> --tier <tier>`. Then resolve with `resolveTier(root, tier, runtime)` — a bare 3-arg call is purpose-scoped to **cell execution** by default (AO12/B1, plan 2A-ii) — (decisions 0012/0015/0019): `inherit` → omit the Agent `model` param AND carry the [bee-tier: ceiling] marker, anchored to the first non-whitespace token of the dispatch prompt or the very start of the description — a marker placed anywhere else never counts (decision 0023, hardened per P1-1 — a bare dispatch with neither param nor an anchored marker is denied by the model-guard hook; ceiling = the session model); `model` → set it; `budget` → state the tier in the prompt as a read budget + output cap and carry the matching [bee-tier: <tier>] marker at that same anchored position; `cli` → for cell execution this now returns `{type:'refused', reason:'cli_tier_gather_only'}` — a cli-shaped tier is not yet proven safe to run a cell's reserve/verify/cap contract against (Discovery-2: an external CLI's cwd is not the repo root), so it stays gated behind W9's absolute-path dogfood. **Re-route the cell to a model tier** (generation or ceiling, per the rubric above) and dispatch natively — never dispatch a cell to a cli-shaped tier. A cli-shaped slot serves **gathers only**, through the Delegation contract's cli gather branch (`resolveTier(root, slot, runtime, {for:'gather'})`, `bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md`), never a cell dispatch. Keep `ceiling` scarce — if `bee_status` flags ceiling scarcity, re-judge routine cells downward before spawning.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-101-
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-102-   **After the tier choice, resolve the advisor slot for this dispatch** (AO4/AO5): `resolveAdvisor(root, runtime)`. The configured advisor IS the advisor — no family test, no strength test, no self-judged skip (AO5); the orchestrator's only judgment is the one honest no-op below, never a hardcoded strength ladder. Add an `Advisor` line to the dispatch (template in `references/swarming-reference.md`) **only** when the advisor resolves AND passes that check:
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-103-   - No advisor configured → skip, no `Advisor` line.
--
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-106-   - When it passes, the `Advisor` line names the advisor identity and states its proven transport verbatim (model-shaped vs cli-shaped, per `references/swarming-reference.md`) — this must match what bee-executing's Advisor Consult section tells the worker to run.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-107-5. **Record workers** before results arrive: `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs state worker add --nickname <n> --cell <id> --tier <tier> --status <status>` per worker.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-108-6. **Tend** the swarm: collect status tokens, update cells and state, verify reservations were released. Silence is not failure — inspect cell status and `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs reservations list --active-only` before assuming a worker is stuck. Do not send routine mid-flight pings; interrupt only for explicit user aborts or confirmed deadlocks.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:109:   For native Codex agents, a completed `wait_agent` call with no completion is an **empty wait** and a timeout signal only. Never follow it directly with another `wait_agent`; authority, urgency, and no-chatter instructions create no exception. Before any later bounded wait, continue material task-local work when any remains; otherwise take exactly one `list_agents` snapshot. Then send one concise commentary update naming both the live agent state and the next action; only then may a later bounded wait run. No-op work, repeated state reads, hidden reasoning, generic commentary, or commentary alone do not qualify. Timeout never licenses interrupt, duplicate dispatch, claim release, or reservation release; every running agent, claim, and reservation stays owned. External process and artifact polling remains governed by the separate executor contract in the reference.
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-110-
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-111-7. **Goal-check every `[DONE]` yourself (P12, decision 0018) — miss reruns, hit ships.** A worker's word is never the evidence; the orchestrator measures before the cell counts:
skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md-112-   - **Re-run the verify.** Run the cell's verify command yourself (fresh output, your own shell). `tiny`/`small` lanes may spot-check one representative cell per wave; `standard`/`high-risk` re-run every behavior-change cell. Failure → the cell is NOT done: re-dispatch to the same tier with the failing output (a task miss is a rerun, never a silent tier escalation — provider errors, not task errors, are what the rescue ladder's tier rung is for).

docs/backlog.md:26:| P44 | A single session fans work into independent-feature git worktrees, each with its own gates, merged back as event-sourced state | New opt-in mode (coexists with P40, does not touch its D2): a worktree runs its own full bee lifecycle — own phase, own gates, own append-only `log/` — and reconciles both code and provenance to `main` on `git merge`. `.bee/` splits into three lifecycle tiers (`log/` tracked+`merge=union`, `cache/` derived-by-`bee replay`, `runtime/` live-never-merged); replay is idempotent (stable event id → sort → dedup). Same-feature/same-file concurrency stays out of scope (claims/holds). Subsumes P30. Proven in beegog first before any host rollout (source: mdview host report + owner direction 2026-07-16; docs/history/worktree-feature-parallelism/CONTEXT.md D1–D6). SHIPPED 2026-07-16: spike GREEN 5/5 → S2 decision module → S4 resolveRoots wire-in (P40 byte-for-byte, test-first) → Slice A `bee worktree register/list/unregister` CLI + bootstrap → Slice B tier merge-safety (.gitattributes merge=union + runtime/cache gitignore). 4 new test suites in commands.verify; full suite green; pushed (1011b52→ffc2b03). Host rollout still deferred (D6). | done | worktree-feature-parallelism |

skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md:12:6. **Baseline gate:** if `.bee/config.json` records `commands.verify`, run it once per session before claiming any cell. A red baseline is surfaced to the user and becomes its own fix-first tiny cell — never build on red. If no commands are recorded, capture the host project's `setup/start/test/verify` into `.bee/config.json` `commands` at the first natural moment (exploring or onboarding).

skills/bee-hive/templates/agents/bee-review.md.tmpl:3:description: Review-class subagent for the bee Delegation contract (skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md) — plan-checking, cell review, exploring's fresh-eyes pass, and bee-reviewing's specialists. Dispatch when a mechanical step needs an independent read-only check against a claim, not a rewrite. May run commands read-only (tests, linters, git diff, the configured verify) to check evidence, but never edits the working tree. Runs at the review tier (falls back to generation when review is unconfigured).

docs/09-harness-course-adoption.md:26:Course, three ways: Fresh Session Test questions 3–4 ("how do I run it? how do I verify it?"), the clock-in/clock-out routine (L05), the fixed 9-step startup flow (steps 5–7: run `init.sh`, run baseline verification, **if baseline is broken fix that first**), and `RELIABILITY.md`'s Standard Paths. bee records per-cell `verify` commands but has **no repo-level record of the host project's setup/start/test/verify commands**, and no session-level baseline check — a fresh bee session can answer "where are we" perfectly and still not know how to start the app.
docs/09-harness-course-adoption.md:29:- `.bee/config.json` gains `"commands": {"setup", "start", "test", "verify"}` — captured at onboarding (prompted) or during first exploring; scribing keeps it current like any Pointers-level fact.
docs/04-skills-spec.md:98:- **Body must cover:** the audit → hunt → propose → execute → close-the-loop cycle; the entropy formula (including the `stale specs` term, decision 0001) and trend reporting; hunt sources (friction clusters, dead code/unused exports, stale docs, stale or missing area specs (proposed sync/harvest work routes through bee-scribing), TODO/stub debris, unverified verify-commands, superseded-but-cited decisions, slop patterns in recent diffs); proposal format (pain / predicted impact / risk lane) with mandatory user approval before any deletion; execution through normal tiny/small cells (reservation + verify + cap — grooming never edits directly); actual-outcome recording; promotion of durable lessons to compounding.

docs/history/learnings/20260717-guard-membership-escape-routes.md:31:**The "exists but nothing runs it" class recurred at cell-planning time (third sighting of the family).** `scripts/test_config_validate.mjs` — the validator's real behavioral suite — was orphaned from `commands.verify` since 2A-i, and the fresh 2B cell initially targeted the wrong test file entirely (`test_lib.mjs`, which only census-checks the export name). The adversarial panel caught both. Rule: **when a cell adds rows to a test suite, the cell names the suite by path AND proves the suite is in the baseline chain — if it is not, joining `commands.verify` (on a green tree) is part of the same cell, never a follow-up.** Companion rule confirmed again: bee's own config must pass bee's own validator — the repo ran red on `bee config validate` for a day and nothing surfaced it because no session ran the verb; it is now exercised by the baseline via the suite.

docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md:42:- **No verify chain and no CI runs any hook suite.** `.bee/config.json` `commands.verify` runs only `test_lib.mjs`, `test_onboard_bee.mjs`, `test_portable_paths.mjs`. **That is why both rots survived** — and why the standing learning of 2026-07-14 (`critical-patterns.md:6-18`) was cited as if it had been applied while a second instance of the exact class stayed live.
docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md:73:Nothing in this feature is verifiable until this lands. A guard change proven by a suite that fail-opens is not proven. **Order matters and is not negotiable** — the verify-chain edit is the **last commit on a green tree, never the first on a red one.** Adding a red suite to `commands.verify` would brick the baseline for every session in every host that syncs the config, and bee's own law then makes the next session's first act "fix the baseline".
docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md:77:3. **`ao-0b`** — only once all three suites are green: add them to `.bee/config.json` `commands.verify`.

docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/plan.md:174:  2. **Sync mechanism (AO10-safe):** a new flat managed-file step inside `onboard_bee.mjs` — NOT `REPO_SKILL_TARGETS` — same class as the settings-json hook merge: byte-render, write, and record `agents_sync: {version, files, rendered_from}` in `.bee/onboarding.json` as its own version marker. docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/reports/review-unreviewed-batch-20260716.md:14:2. **Operative Codex spawn doc taught the retired API; the ORCH-01 census was red and unwired** (architecture + test-coverage). `swarming-reference.md:120,123,125` used `spawn_agent(agent_type=…, fork_context=…)` and re-spawn prose, forbidden by SPEC ORCH-01; `census_stale_spawn_syntax.mjs` exited 1 with 4 violations and was absent from `commands.verify`. **Fix (cell `codex-harness-hardening-6`):** table rewritten to `spawn_agent({task_name, message, fork_turns:"none"})` / `followup_task({target, message})`; projections synced; census green and wired as verify suite #16.
docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/reports/review-unreviewed-batch-20260716.md:35:No guard weakened, no test deleted/softened anywhere in the range — `commands.verify` grew 6 → 16 suites, each new guard carries a biting self-test. No injection surface, no secret reads, no credential logging; the security-relevant changes are hardening (downgrade block fail-closed and unforceable, source classifier fail-closed, sha256 manifest check, machine-checked bypass doctrine). Public CLI surface changes are additive and backward-compatible; plugin manifests only bump version; templates ↔ runtime byte-parity holds for every touched lib file.

docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/reports/ao-2b-2.md:5:**Outcome:** `validateModelsConfig` (per AO8) now refuses a write-granting sandbox token on an ADVICE-CLASS cli slot (`advisor`, `review` — every runtime) with a new `cli-advice-slot-writable` code. Blocklist: `-s workspace-write`, `--sandbox workspace-write`, `--sandbox=workspace-write`, bare `danger-full-access` (honestly framed as known-bad tokens, not a positive read-only guarantee). `generation`/`extraction` cli slots are untouched — verified with a dedicated discriminator row. `danger-full-access` on an advice slot reports both the universal `cli-unsafe-flag` code and the new one. `.bee/config.json` migrated: `promptVia:"stdin"` added to `models.claude.advisor` and `models.codex.review`, and `scripts/test_config_validate.mjs` joined `commands.verify` (added only after confirming a green tree, ao-0b law). `bee config validate --json` now ends `{ok:true, problem_count:0}`. Mirror `.bee/bin/lib/state.mjs` stays byte-identical. Release manifest regenerated via `--write`.

docs/history/p49-force-downgrade-blast-radius/reports/advisor-digest.md:111:**Baseline gate (docs/09 item 1):** if `.bee/config.json` records `commands.verify`, run it once per session before any cell is claimed. A red baseline is surfaced to the user and becomes its own fix-first tiny cell — never build on red. Commands come free in the session preamble; when none are recorded, `bee_status` warns and the capture belongs to exploring or onboarding, never to guesswork.
docs/history/p49-force-downgrade-blast-radius/reports/advisor-digest.md:704:**Greenfield init lane (P1, docs/09 item 6):** when the repo has no build and the init-lane offer was accepted at onboarding, the first slice is **one init cell** — `must_haves`: setup succeeds from scratch, one passing test exists, standard commands recorded in `.bee/config.json`, clean first commit — before any feature cell. Infrastructure first; the init cell's verify command is the recorded `test` command itself.

     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	// Guard: commands.verify in .bee/config.json must always run every mandatory
     3	// suite. TEST-11/D-14 (SPEC §11.2, decision ed0b2920): the CLI registry
     4	// contract test (test_bee_cli.mjs) is a public-command contract and must
     5	// never silently fall out of the release verify chain again — this script
     6	// is the mechanical guard that catches that regression at verify time.
     7	
     8	import fs from "node:fs";
     9	import path from "node:path";
    10	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
    11	
    12	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    13	const REPO_ROOT = path.join(__dirname, "..");
    14	const CONFIG_PATH = path.join(REPO_ROOT, ".bee", "config.json");
    15	
    16	// The suites that MUST always run as part of commands.verify.
    17	const MANDATORY_SUITES = [
    18	  "test_lib",
    19	  "test_onboard_bee",
    20	  "test_plugin_distribution",
    21	  "test_portable_paths",
    22	  "test_model_guard",
    23	  "test_write_guard",
    24	  "test_hook_contracts",
    25	  "test_bee_cli",
    26	  "test_installers_e2e",
    27	];
    28	
    29	/**
    30	 * Checks a verify command string for every mandatory suite name.
    31	 * Returns { ok, missing } — missing is the list of suite names (in
    32	 * MANDATORY_SUITES order) that do not appear anywhere in verifyString.
    33	 */
    34	function checkVerifyString(verifyString) {
    35	  const haystack = typeof verifyString === "string" ? verifyString : "";
    36	  const missing = MANDATORY_SUITES.filter((suite) => !haystack.includes(suite));
    37	  return { ok: missing.length === 0, missing };
    38	}
    39	
    40	// ─── internal self-test: prove the checker actually bites ─────────────────
    41	// Feed the checker a synthetic verify-string with test_bee_cli removed (built
    42	// from the mandatory list itself, never from the real config) and assert it
    43	// is flagged missing. This never mutates the real .bee/config.json — it only
    44	// proves checkVerifyString() is not a rubber stamp before trusting it below.
    45	{
    46	  const syntheticSuites = MANDATORY_SUITES.filter((s) => s !== "test_bee_cli");
    47	  const syntheticVerify = syntheticSuites
    48	    .map((s) => `node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/${s}.mjs`)
    49	    .join(" && ");
    50	
    51	  const selfTestResult = checkVerifyString(syntheticVerify);
    52	
    53	  if (selfTestResult.ok || !selfTestResult.missing.includes("test_bee_cli")) {
    54	    console.error(
    55	      "FAIL test_verify_manifest: internal self-test did not catch a synthetic verify-string with test_bee_cli removed",
    56	    );
    57	    console.error(`      synthetic verify-string: ${syntheticVerify}`);
    58	    console.error(`      checker result: ${JSON.stringify(selfTestResult)}`);
    59	    process.exit(1);
    60	  }
    61	
    62	  console.log("PASS test_verify_manifest: internal self-test — checker correctly flags a synthetic verify-string missing test_bee_cli");
    63	}
    64	
    65	// ─── real check: THIS repo's real .bee/config.json ─────────────────────────
    66	
    67	if (!fs.existsSync(CONFIG_PATH)) {
    68	  console.error(`FAIL test_verify_manifest: ${CONFIG_PATH} does not exist`);
    69	  process.exit(1);
    70	}
    71	
    72	let config;
    73	try {
    74	  config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, "utf8"));
    75	} catch (error) {
    76	  console.error(`FAIL test_verify_manifest: could not parse ${CONFIG_PATH}: ${error.message}`);
    77	  process.exit(1);
    78	}
    79	
    80	const verifyString = config?.commands?.verify;
    81	if (typeof verifyString !== "string" || !verifyString.trim()) {
    82	  console.error("FAIL test_verify_manifest: .bee/config.json commands.verify is missing or empty");
    83	  process.exit(1);
    84	}
    85	
    86	const result = checkVerifyString(verifyString);
    87	if (!result.ok) {
    88	  console.error(`FAIL test_verify_manifest: commands.verify is missing ${result.missing.length} mandatory suite(s): ${result.missing.join(", ")}`);
    89	  console.error(`      commands.verify: ${verifyString}`);
    90	  process.exit(1);
    91	}
    92	
    93	console.log(`PASS test_verify_manifest: commands.verify contains all ${MANDATORY_SUITES.length} mandatory suites (${MANDATORY_SUITES.join(", ")})`);

codex
A second, more fundamental issue emerged: today an installed project copy can safely act as the next onboarding source only because it is a lossless byte mirror. Once markers are stripped, a Codex projection no longer contains the Claude branch (and vice versa), so it cannot refresh both managed roots. That source-authority problem must be solved before the renderer can be considered feasible.

docs/specs/onboarding.md-2-area: onboarding
docs/specs/onboarding.md-3-updated: 2026-07-16
docs/specs/onboarding.md-4-coverage: partial
docs/specs/onboarding.md-5-sources:
docs/specs/onboarding.md:6:  - installer-version-parity-1-3-1 locked rules (fail-closed release tuple, full projection parity, greenfield/brownfield end-to-end success contract; D1/D3 verified in-engine with plugin-first coverage, D7 managed-set cleanup fencing, D2/D8 Linux Bash E2E shipped — cells -4/-2/-3, 2026-07-16; field fix cell -5: plugin CLI mutation verbs take NO --json (only `plugin list` does — real codex/claude contract), rollback reconciles the probed current state against the pre-run snapshot and reports failure only when restoring a previously-present plugin genuinely fails (a transition that died before installing anything rolls back as a no-op success), and the E2E fake CLIs reject --json on mutations so the wrong-flag contract can never test green again; field fix cell -6: legacy global skill copies (the pre-1.0 `~/.claude/skills` layout) are refreshed IN PLACE on every onboard — refresh-only over managed bee-* dirs that already exist there (never create, never delete, non-managed dirs untouched, resolved-newer global never downgraded, self/overlap sources skipped), reported as `refresh_legacy_global_skill` plan items that never drive `up_to_date`/blocked-first aggregation — closing the two-version skill inconsistency where a repo at the current release coexisted with a stale global copy both loaded by the runtime)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-7-  - codex-sandbox-baseline cells codex-sandbox-baseline-1/codex-sandbox-baseline-2 (real onboarding entrypoint through the shared isolated test runner; full onboarding suite green, 2026-07-16)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-8-  - codex-hook-state-parity cells 2, 3, 5 (paired Codex lifecycle audit, exclusive plugin-first/repo-copy distribution, and fresh-host handler delivery; capped traces and reports, 2026-07-16)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-9-  - codex-harness-hardening-1d cells 1d-1/1d-2 (SRC-01..06 source-identity classifier R17 + status source field; 8 classifier/status tests, 2026-07-15)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-10-  - codex-harness-hardening-1c cell codex-harness-hardening-1c-1 (honest status drift R16 via the onboarding managed-hash ledger; 5 drift tests, 2026-07-15)
--
docs/specs/onboarding.md-20-  - bee-footprint D1 (managed ignore section, cells footprint-1/footprint-4, 2026-07-12)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-21-  - fanout-delegation D1 (stale advisor key tolerance, 2026-07-12)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-22-  - sticky-repo-hooks (cell sticky-hooks-1, 2026-07-13; found auditing 8 host projects after the v0.1.30 rollout)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-23-decisions:
docs/specs/onboarding.md:24:  - 55ff17ef (release-version parity is fail-closed across every distributed projection)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-25-  - 09b776b5 (both installers prove complete greenfield/brownfield postconditions before success)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-26-  - fc76ce41 (release 1.3.1 excludes unfinished wait-loop/worktree-isolation work)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-27-  - 17bfc14a (Codex-safe onboarding tests preserve the real CLI entrypoint and observable process contract through an isolated Worker)
docs/specs/onboarding.md-28-  - a83a3613 (shared isolated runner for nested Node entrypoints; real Git/Bash/Codex integration remains external)
--
docs/specs/onboarding.md-192-default on a capable runtime. It proves an enabled installed package and its
docs/specs/onboarding.md-193-complete release inventory, preflights the whole transaction, then removes only
docs/specs/onboarding.md-194-direct plain `bee-*` skill directories and catalog-recognized bee hook entries
docs/specs/onboarding.md-195-from project fallback roots. Repo-copy first proves the package inactive, then
docs/specs/onboarding.md:196:generates the managed project projections; Codex-only receives the same hook
docs/specs/onboarding.md-197-catalog as a combined-runtime install. Bash and PowerShell installers use the
docs/specs/onboarding.md-198-same planner and proof rules. A symlink, alias, unknown target, invalid ledger,
docs/specs/onboarding.md-199-package mismatch, or hook-shape mismatch aborts before any write. Release proof
docs/specs/onboarding.md-200-uses a staged cachebuster without changing the canonical package/version tuple;
docs/specs/onboarding.md-201-live user-home installation and fresh-thread loading remain outstanding UAT.

docs/specs/onboarding.md-204-file delivery is a failed install, even when catalog parity itself is green.

--
docs/specs/onboarding.md-271-carries a forbidden character, a reserved device name, or a trailing dot/space.
docs/specs/onboarding.md-272-
docs/specs/onboarding.md-273-**Wire the second-runtime guards (repo-hook installs).** Trigger: any run for a
docs/specs/onboarding.md-274-project that vendors repo-local hooks (the explicit opt-in flag or its sticky
docs/specs/onboarding.md:275:record). What blocks it: nothing — the projection is derived from the same hook
docs/specs/onboarding.md-276-catalog as the first runtime's wiring. What changes: the second runtime's
docs/specs/onboarding.md-277-project hook file is created or merged so every guarded lifecycle event
docs/specs/onboarding.md-278-(session start, prompt, pre-write guard, post-task sync, subagent close,
docs/specs/onboarding.md-279-pre-compaction, session close) runs the same vendored guard scripts. Merge

docs/specs/onboarding.md-338-  (decision c6ee6b6e, review finding P2-3).
docs/specs/onboarding.md-339-- **R5** — Plugin-capable runtimes
docs/specs/onboarding.md-340-  receive bee primarily as one installable package containing the shared workflow
docs/specs/onboarding.md-341-  skills and compatible lifecycle hooks. Release and reinstall update the package
docs/specs/onboarding.md:342:  the runtime loads directly; project-local skill and hook projections remain an
docs/specs/onboarding.md-343-  explicit fallback and dogfood route. One installation activates exactly one
docs/specs/onboarding.md-344-  source so a skill or event never runs twice (decision 4cc1c355, extended by
docs/specs/onboarding.md-345-  codex-hook-state-parity D9; decision cf511ff3).
docs/specs/onboarding.md-346-- **R6** — On Codex, every lifecycle capability
--
docs/specs/onboarding.md-420-- **R17** — Source origin is named, never guessed from the nearest path. A single
docs/specs/onboarding.md-421-  shared detector classifies the bee source tree into exactly one of five
docs/specs/onboarding.md-422-  origins: a **canonical development checkout** (the authored source, carrying its
docs/specs/onboarding.md-423-  package manifest and version-control marker), a **project's vendored copy** (a
docs/specs/onboarding.md:424:  projection living inside a host project's assistant-runtime folders), an
docs/specs/onboarding.md-425-  **installed package** (a distributed manifested snapshot — usable as the source
docs/specs/onboarding.md-426-  for the same project's runtime and copies, but never permitted to install
docs/specs/onboarding.md-427-  shared/global targets), the **legacy shared location** (the old machine-global
docs/specs/onboarding.md-428-  root — only reported or migrated, never an implicit source), and **unrecognized**
--

     1	// source-identity.mjs — SRC-01..06: classify the running launcher's source
     2	// identity, so onboarding and `bee status` share ONE detector (DIST-04) instead
     3	// of each guessing from the nearest path (SRC-01).
     4	//
     5	// PURE: only read probes (existsSync / realpathSync / readFileSync). It never
     6	// mutates anything and never throws — any probe failure, unparseable manifest,
     7	// or ambiguity resolves to `unknown` (fail-closed, SRC-04). Classification is a
     8	// report/corroboration surface; onboarding's authoritative-source refusal
     9	// (identityOk) remains the decision-maker.
    10	
    11	import fs from 'node:fs';
    12	import path from 'node:path';
    13	
    14	export const SOURCE_KINDS = [
    15	  'source_checkout', // the canonical dev working checkout (plugin.json + .git)
    16	  'project_projection', // a host repo's vendored .agents/skills or .claude/skills copy
    17	  'plugin_package', // an installed manifested snapshot (plugin.json, no .git) — never a global/plugin authority (SRC-03)
    18	  'legacy_global', // the legacy global ~/.claude/skills root — reported/migrated, never an implicit source (SRC-06)
    19	  'unknown', // missing/unparseable manifest or ambiguous — fail closed before mutation (SRC-04)
    20	];
    21	
    22	function realpathOrNull(p) {
    23	  try {
    24	    return fs.realpathSync(p);
    25	  } catch {
    26	    return null;
    27	  }
    28	}
    29	
    30	function existsSafe(p) {
    31	  try {
    32	    return fs.existsSync(p);
    33	  } catch {
    34	    return false;
    35	  }
    36	}
    37	
    38	/**
    39	 * Classify the source identity of a running launcher.
    40	 * @param {{ hiveDir: string, homeDir?: string }} input
    41	 *   hiveDir — the running launcher's `.../bee-hive` directory.
    42	 *   homeDir — the user's home dir (for the legacy global-root check); optional.
    43	 * @returns {{ kind: string, root: string|null, markers: object }}
    44	 */
    45	export function classifySource({ hiveDir, homeDir } = {}) {
    46	  if (!hiveDir || typeof hiveDir !== 'string') {
    47	    return { kind: 'unknown', root: null, markers: { reason: 'no hiveDir' } };
    48	  }
    49	  const sourceRoot = path.dirname(hiveDir); // .../skills (or .agents/skills, .claude/skills)
    50	  const pluginRoot = path.dirname(sourceRoot); // the package / repo root
    51	  const markers = { source_root: sourceRoot, plugin_root: pluginRoot };
    52	
    53	  // (1) legacy_global FIRST — the global ~/.claude/skills root also has a
    54	  // `.claude` grandparent, so it would collide with project_projection below;
    55	  // the realpath match to the true global root disambiguates it.
    56	  if (homeDir && typeof homeDir === 'string') {
    57	    const globalRoot = path.join(homeDir, '.claude', 'skills');
    58	    const rp = realpathOrNull(sourceRoot);
    59	    const rpGlobal = realpathOrNull(globalRoot);
    60	    if (rp && rpGlobal && rp === rpGlobal) {
    61	      return { kind: 'legacy_global', root: pluginRoot, markers: { ...markers, global_root: globalRoot } };
    62	    }
    63	  }
    64	
    65	  // (2) project_projection — launcher under a host's .agents/skills or .claude/skills.
    66	  const projectionParent = path.basename(pluginRoot);
    67	  if (projectionParent === '.agents' || projectionParent === '.claude') {
    68	    return { kind: 'project_projection', root: pluginRoot, markers: { ...markers, projection_parent: projectionParent } };
    69	  }
    70	
    71	  // (3)/(4) a manifested package: .claude-plugin/plugin.json at the package root.
    72	  const pluginManifest = path.join(pluginRoot, '.claude-plugin', 'plugin.json');
    73	  if (existsSafe(pluginManifest)) {
    74	    // SRC-04: an unparseable manifest is `unknown`, never a usable source.
    75	    try {
    76	      JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(pluginManifest, 'utf8'));
    77	    } catch {
    78	      return { kind: 'unknown', root: pluginRoot, markers: { ...markers, reason: 'plugin.json unparseable' } };
    79	    }
    80	    if (existsSafe(path.join(pluginRoot, '.git'))) {
    81	      return { kind: 'source_checkout', root: pluginRoot, markers: { ...markers, plugin_manifest: true, git: true } };
    82	    }
    83	    // plugin.json without .git — a distributed snapshot. SRC-03: it may source
    84	    // the same repo's runtime + projection, but is NEVER a global/plugin-target
    85	    // authority.
    86	    return {
    87	      kind: 'plugin_package',
    88	      root: pluginRoot,
    89	      markers: { ...markers, plugin_manifest: true, git: false, can_target_global: false },
    90	    };
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  // (5) unknown — no manifest, not a projection, not the global root: fail closed.
    94	  return { kind: 'unknown', root: pluginRoot, markers: { ...markers, reason: 'no plugin manifest' } };
    95	}
52:import { classifySource } from "../templates/lib/source-identity.mjs";
396:  let sourceKind = "unknown";
398:    sourceKind = classifySource({ hiveDir: HIVE_DIR, homeDir: os.homedir() }).kind;
405:  if (sourceKind === "project_projection" || sourceKind === "legacy_global") {
2770:function sourceKindForReport() {
2772:    return classifySource({ hiveDir: HIVE_DIR, homeDir: os.homedir() }).kind;
2830:        source: sourceKindForReport(),

codex
1. **BLOCKER — rendered projections become lossy but are still accepted as onboarding sources.** A project projection is explicitly classified and accepted as a valid source ([source-identity.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/source-identity.mjs:65), [onboard_bee.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs:401)). Today this works because both roots are complete byte mirrors. After filtering, a Codex projection cannot reconstruct Claude-only content, yet it would still try to sync both roots. Resolve this by preserving a lossless canonical payload in a non-loaded location, requiring a canonical/plugin source for cross-runtime sync, or redesigning projections so they remain reversible. The current “no new layout” constraint is incompatible with lossless self-upgrade.

2. **BLOCKER — plugin-first users will load both runtime branches.** The Codex manifest points directly at raw `./skills/`, and the release inventory packages canonical `skills/` bytes ([plugin.json](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:10), [release_manifest.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/scripts/release_manifest.mjs:118)). HTML comments hide only the marker lines; Markdown between them remains visible, and a raw-text skill loader sees everything anyway. Therefore deferring plugin pre-rendering creates the exact cross-runtime instruction bleed D9 is meant to remove. Plugin rendering must land in this slice, or tagging must wait.

3. **MAJOR — use three distinct integrity contracts.**

   - Release/package integrity: hash canonical source bytes, including markers.
   - Per-target drift: compare each installed file against `render(canonicalBytes, targetRuntime)`, never raw source.
   - Downgrade protection: continue comparing semantic release versions. The current three-version preflight is version-based ([onboard_bee.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs:829)); hashes must not replace it.

   Add target provenance/render-schema metadata so a same-version projection cannot masquerade as a complete source for the opposite runtime. Map `repo-claude`, `global`, and `legacy-global` to Claude rendering; `repo-agents` to Codex rendering.

4. **MAJOR — the test/cell scope is incomplete.** The existing `test_lib.mjs` census requires Codex `wait_agent` prose in `.claude/skills`, so correct tagging will deliberately break it ([test_lib.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs:7879)). None of the three cells owns that file. Also, `test_skill_render.mjs` is absent from the 23-command repository verification chain and from its mandatory-suite guard ([config.json](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/.bee/config.json:13), [test_verify_manifest.mjs](/home/thanhsmind/projects/goglbe/beegog/scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs:16)). Add these surfaces to cell scope and make the renderer test permanently mandatory.

   Expected suite effects:

   - `test_lib_mirror`: should remain unchanged; it does not compare skill projections.
   - `test_plugin_distribution`: likely remains green, which is a coverage gap because it proves raw package inventory, not runtime-clean plugin loading.
   - `release_manifest --check`: will fail after canonical skill/renderer edits until deliberately regenerated.
   - Existing no-marker onboarding equality tests should remain green byte-for-byte. Marker fixtures must compare target-rendered bytes instead of raw-source hashes.
   - Release-tuple and downgrade tests should remain version-based and green.

5. **MAJOR — marker grammar and failure atomicity need tightening.** Reserve exact full-line markers and refuse nesting, unclosed blocks, stray `bee:end`, unknown runtime labels, markers before/inside YAML frontmatter, and ambiguous near-markers. Either forbid the literal markers inside fenced examples or implement a fully tested fence-aware parser; forbidding them is safer. Preserve BOM, CRLF, final-newline state, and arbitrary bytes on the zero-marker path. Validate the complete source tree before any apply mutation—not merely “zero writes for that skill”—so one malformed late skill cannot leave an installation partially updated.

6. **MAJOR — “rendered union equals source” and token spot-greps are insufficient.** Shared text is duplicated across both renders, so a set-like union is not a meaningful equality proof. Freeze pre-tag canonical bytes, then assert that removing marker lines from the tagged source reproduces that baseline exactly, except for the explicitly allowed D10 delta. Separately compare both ordered render outputs byte-for-byte against parser-derived expectations.

7. **Attribution rule — classify by who must act, not who is mentioned.** The AO11 statement “Codex has no per-agent subagent type; enforce tier through read/output budgets” is operational guidance for a Codex orchestrator and should be Codex-only. A Claude orchestrator using a configured Codex CLI follows the shared CLI-shaped transport contract; it does not need Codex-native `spawn_agent` mechanics. Keep only genuinely cross-runtime invariants shared, with each runtime’s action mechanics in its own block. If a contrast is useful to humans, place it in non-loaded documentation rather than both runtime skill projections.

**Verdict: RETURN-TO-PLANNING.**

The renderer itself is implementable, but slice 3 cannot safely proceed until the lossy-source/self-upgrade problem and raw plugin-source bleed are resolved, and the verification scope is expanded accordingly. No files or bee state were modified.
